Analyses reveal the presence of **ribose and glucose sugars, amino acids, nucleobases, nitrogen-rich polymers,** **and abundant presolar grains from supernovae**, strengthening the idea that asteroids could have delivered key ingredients for life to early Earth.
● Scientists' findings lend strong support to the **RNA World Hypothesis**, which proposes that **early life** **forms relied on RNA for both storing genetic information and carrying out catalytic functions,** **before the evolution of DNA and proteins.** The study’s authors suggest that the widespread occurrence of asteroids like Bennu in the inner solar system could have supplied essential sugars and amino acids, ultimately contributing to the emergence of life on Earth over 3.5 billion years ago. **Therefore, option (c) is the correct answer.**
**Relevance:** Recently, NASA’s research on asteroid Bennu has revealed the presence of fundamental building blocks of life.

questions.subject: Science & Technology
questions.questionText:
Consider the following statements with regard to
gravitational microlensing:
I. 	 It can be used to detect celestial objects that
emit or reflect too little light to be observed by conventional telescopes.
II. 	 It can be used to calculate the physical mass
of dense, “dead” stars such as white dwarfs.
III. 	It provides high-resolution surface images
of exoplanets similar to direct imaging techniques.
Which of the statements given above are correct?
questions.explanationMarkdown:
● **Gravitational microlensing** is a subtle form of gravitational lensing in which the gravity of an object bends and briefly magnifies the light from a background star, due to the curvature of space-time caused by mass. While strong lensing occurs near massive objects like galaxies or black holes, even **smaller** **bodies such as stars and planets can produce a detectable microlensing signal.** This technique is especially useful for detecting faint, dark, or isolated objects, such as rogue planets and stellar remnants, by analysing the temporary brightening of the background star to infer the properties of the intervening object.
● Microlensing can reveal objects that **emit little or no light**, such as rogue planets, brown dwarfs and black holes, because detection depends on their **gravitational effect on background starlight,** not on their own brightness. **So, statement I is correct.**
● The pattern and duration of a microlensing event can be used to estimate the **mass of the lensing object,** including dense stellar remnants like **white dwarfs**, neutron stars and black holes. **So, statement II is** **correct.**
● Microlensing **does not produce resolved or high-resolution images** of exoplanet surfaces. It only provides indirect information through changes in brightness, unlike direct imaging methods that attempt to capture actual light from the planet. **So, statement III is not correct.** **Therefore, option (a) is the correct answer.**
**Relevance:** A study recently used microlensing to weigh a rogue planet

questions.subject: Science & Technology
questions.microTopic: Information and Communication Technology, Robotics, AI
questions.questionText:
Consider the following pairs:
Sensor Function
I. - Gyroscope Measures linear acceleration and tilt
II. - Accelerometer Detects the angular rotation of the device
III. - Magnetometer Provides directional orientation
IV. - Proximity Sensor
Determines altitude using air pressure
How many of the pairs given above are correctly matched?
questions.explanationMarkdown:
● A **gyroscope** does not measure linear acceleration or tilt. Instead, it measures **angular velocity**, i.e., how fast and in which direction a device is rotating. In smartphones, gyroscopes are micro-electromechanical sensors that detect rotation using a vibrating mass whose motion changes when the device rotates. **So,** **pair I is not correctly matched.**
● An **accelerometer** measures **linear acceleration**, including acceleration due to gravity. It allows a smartphone to detect **tilt, orientation, and movement**, such as knowing which way is “up” and rotating the screen accordingly. **So, pair II is not correctly matched.**
● A **magnetometer** measures the **Earth’s magnetic field** and functions as a **digital compass** in smartphones. It helps determine the device’s **directional orientation**, such as identifying north, south, east, and west, and is commonly used in navigation and map applications. **So, pair III is correctly** **matched.**
● A **proximity sensor** is used to detect the presence of nearby objects, such as turning off the phone’s display when it is held close to the ear during a call. **Altitude measurement using air pressure** is performed by a **barometer**, not a proximity sensor. **So, pair IV is not correctly matched.** **So, only one of the pairs given above is correctly matched.** **Therefore, option (a) is the correct answer.**

questions.subject: Agriculture
questions.questionText:
Consider the following statements:
I. 	 India is the only country in the world that
cultivates all four commercially grown cotton species.
II. 	 Maharashtra was the largest producer of
cotton in India in the year 2024-2025.
III. 	Cotton procurement under the MSP regime
is carried out through a strictly open-ended mechanism.
Which of the statements given above are correct?
questions.explanationMarkdown:
● **Cotton** is one of the most important fibres and cash crops of India and plays a vital role in both the agricultural and industrial economy. It provides the primary raw material for the cotton textile industry. **There are four cultivated species of cotton:**
○ **Gossypium arboreum** (Indian cotton) ○ **Gossypium herbaceum** (Levant cotton) ○ **Gossypium hirsutum** (American or upland cotton) ○ **Gossypium barbadense** (Egyptian or extra-long staple cotton)
● Among these, Gossypium hirsutum (American cotton) is the most widely grown species and accounts for **about 90% of global production.** India is the **only country in the world** where **all four cultivated** **species are grown** commercially. **So, statement I is correct.**
● According to provisional estimates of the **Committee on Cotton Production and Consumption** for the **2024–25 season,** India’s total cotton output is pegged at 294.25 lakh bales. Among the states, **Maharashtra continues to dominate**, producing about 89.09 lakh bales, a significant rise from 80.45 lakh bales in 2023–24. **Gujarat ranks second**, recording a production of 71.34 lakh bales, though lower than the previous season’s 90.57 lakh bales. **So, statement II is correct.**
● The Government of India announces a **Minimum Support Price (MSP)** for cotton each year to protect farmers when market prices fall. The **Cotton Corporation of India (CCI) procures cotton at MSP when** **necessary** and sells its stocks through a transparent online e-auction platform, ensuring fair price discovery and competitive supply to the textile industry. MSP acts as a safety net to prevent distress sales. However, unlike rice and wheat, cotton procurement **is not strictly open-ended**, though there are no fixed quantity limits during procurement operations. **So, statement III is not correct.**
**Therefore, option (a) is the correct answer.**
**Relevance:** As the government of India has chosen not to extend the 11% import duty exemption on cotton beyond December 2025, domestic cotton prices are showing an upward trend.

questions.subject: Geography
questions.questionText:
With reference to the physical geography of
Venezuela, consider the following statements:
I. 	 The Equator passes through Lake Maracaibo
located within it.
II. 	 The Andes Mountains extend into its western
region.
III. 	The Amazon biome stretches into its southern
region.
Which of the statements given above is/are
correct?
questions.explanationMarkdown:
● **Lake Maracaibo,** situated in **northwestern** **Venezuela**, is one of South America’s largest inland water bodies. It lies within the Maracaibo Basin and is connected to the **Gulf of Venezuela** and ultimately, the **Caribbean Sea by a narrow strait** **at its northern end.** Although often called a lake, Maracaibo’s hydrology is influenced by its connection to the sea, resulting in brackish waters in the north. **Venezuela’s territory lies entirely in the** **Northern Hemisphere,** between the Equator and approximately 12° N latitude, so the **Equator does** **not intersect Lake Maracaibo. So, statement I** **is not correct.**
● The Andes is the longest continental mountain range in the world, extending along the western edge of South America for about 7,000 km and spanning seven countries: **Venezuela, Colombia,** **Ecuador, Peru, Bolivia, Chile and Argentina.** In Venezuela, the **northernmost portion** of this great fold mountain system is represented by the **Cordillera de Mérida**, which rises in the northwest and forms the country’s **highest** **mountainous region. So, statement II is correct.**
● The **Amazon biome** is one of the world’s largest and most biodiverse tropical ecosystems, spanning parts of eight South American countries, including Brazil, Peru, Colombia, Ecuador, Bolivia, Guyana, Suriname and **Venezuela**, as well as the overseas territory of French Guiana. A significant portion of **southern Venezuela lies within this** **biome,** where Amazonian rainforests and related ecosystems extend into the southernmost states. **So, statement III is correct.** **Therefore, option (b) is the correct answer.**
**Relevance:** The recent U.S. attack on Venezuela is unlikely to have any direct impact on India’s energy security.

questions.subject: International Relations
questions.microTopic: International Current Affairs Focus (Summits, Reports, Conflicts, Visits, etc.)
questions.questionText:
Recently, the first International Spice Routes
Conference was held in which one of the following
Indian cities?
questions.explanationMarkdown:
● The **first International Spice Routes Conference was held in Kochi, Kerala**, at the historic **Bolgatty** **Palace**, a 1744 structure **originally built by Dutch traders.** The three-day conference brought together historians, archaeologists, researchers, policymakers, tourism stakeholders and cultural practitioners, along with 38 delegates from 22 countries. The event sought to revisit the legacy of the ancient spice trade and examine its continuing cultural, commercial and diplomatic relevance.
● The discussions centred on Kerala’s historical position as a crossroads of global maritime exchange. Between the 1st century BC and 4th century AD, the ancient port of **Muziris, located near the mouth of** **the Periyar River**, functioned as a major hub linking Southeast Asia, Africa, West Asia and Europe. It exported pepper, beads and silk while importing gold coins and grain. The conference highlighted how these trade networks facilitated deep cultural exchanges, influencing faith, language, food systems and social identities across regions.
● The event was organised by the **Muziris Heritage Project in collaboration with the Kerala Tourism** **Department.** Beyond historical reflection, the objective was forward-looking: to leverage the cultural, religious and commercial potential of ancient networks for heritage conservation and responsible tourism. **Therefore, option (c) is the correct answer.**
**Relevance:** First International Spice Routes Conference was held in Kochi in January 2026.

questions.subject: Science & Technology
questions.questionText:
Recently, the “Spritacular Project” was seen in the
news. It is primarily associated with which of the
following?

options.a:
Persistent low-level lightning discharges
occurring within thunderclouds
options.b:
Transient luminous electrical events occurring
above thunderstorms
options.c:
Solar wind-induced magnetic storms affecting
Earth’s ionosphere
options.d:
High-altitude ice crystal formations
responsible for noctilucent clouds

questions.explanationMarkdown:
● **National Aeronautics and Space Administration's** **(NASA’s)** newly launched citizen science initiative, **Spritacular project,** harnesses crowdsourcing to deepen scientific understanding of **sprites and other Transient** **Luminous Events (TLEs)**, **short-lived electrical flashes** **that occur above thunderstorms.** The project connects professional researchers with members of the public whose camera recordings can directly support scientific investigations. By building the world’s first crowdsourced, openly accessible database of sprites and related TLEs, Spritacular project aims to significantly expand research capabilities in this field. The most frequently observed TLEs are called **“sprites,”** like the enigmatic and playful fairies of folklore.
● **Sprites** typically form about **50 miles (80 km) above Earth’s surface**, appearing just moments after a lightning strike as sudden reddish flashes. They can take diverse shapes, often featuring **diffuse plumes** **and bright, spiny tendril**s and sometimes seem to **“dance” above storms,** flickering on and off in rapid succession. Despite decades of observation, many aspects of their formation and behaviour remain poorly understood. **Therefore, option (b) is the correct answer.**
**Relevance:** Recently, NASA announced Spritacular, a citizen science initiative that empowers volunteers to photograph transient luminous events.

questions.subject: International Relations
questions.microTopic: International Current Affairs Focus (Summits, Reports, Conflicts, Visits, etc.)
questions.questionText:
Which one of the following countries has
introduced the four interlinked global initiatives,
Global Governance Initiative (GGI), Global
Development Initiative (GDI), Global Security
Initiative (GSI) and Global Civilisation Initiative
(GCI), aimed at addressing contemporary
challenges and advancing reforms in the global
governance system?
questions.explanationMarkdown:
● In **2025,** **Chinese President Xi Jinping’s** presentation of the **Global Governance Initiative (GGI)** at the **Shanghai Cooperation Organization’s Tianjin Summit** represented a landmark moment in global diplomacy. Along with China’s earlier proposals of the **Global Development Initiative (GDI),** the **Global Security Initiative (GSI)** and the **Global Civilization Initiative (GCI),** these four initiatives collectively constitute a comprehensive set of global public goods aimed at addressing contemporary challenges and advancing reforms in the global governance system.
● While each initiative has a distinct focus, they are mutually reinforcing: promoting prosperity through shared development, ensuring stability through common security, fostering mutual trust through exchanges among civilizations and pursuing the greater common good through improved governance.
○ The **GDI** emphasises a people-centred approach that prioritises livelihoods, human rights and inclusive development. ○ The **GSI** stresses resolving differences through dialogue and consultation, while respecting each country’s independently chosen development path and social system. ○ The **GCI** advocates the promotion of common human values and supports all peoples in pursuing peace, development, fairness, justice, democracy and freedom in ways suited to their own national contexts. **Therefore, option (a) is the correct answer.**
**Relevance:** Recently, China has launched four global initiatives that place a strong emphasis on a people-centred vision aimed at promoting peace and shared well-being.

questions.subject: Polity
questions.sectionGroup: Part V & VI: The Union and The States
questions.questionText:
Consider the following statements with regard to
the procedure of removal of judges of the higher
judiciary in India:
I. 	 As per the Constitution of India, a judge can
only be removed for proved misbehaviour or
incapacity.
II. 	 If the motion for removal is admitted in
any house, the Speaker and Chairman must
jointly constitute a three-member committee
to investigate the complaint.
III. 	The motion for removal is required to be
adopted by a majority of at least two-thirds
of the members of that House present and
voting.
Which of the statements given above is/are
correct?
questions.explanationMarkdown:
**Answer**: **a** **Explanation**:
● The removal (impeachment) of a Judge of the Supreme Court of India is provided under **Articles 124(4)** **and 124(5)** of the Constitution of India, while the removal of a High Court Judge is governed by **Articles** **217(1)(b) and 218**.
○ The Constitution stipulates that a judge of the Supreme Court or a High Court can be removed from office only by an order of the President. Such an order can be issued only after an address has been passed by each House of Parliament, in the same session, **on the grounds of proved** **misbehaviour or incapacity. So, statement I is correct.**
● The detailed procedural aspects for the removal of judges is elaborated in the **Judges Inquiry Act, 1968**. Under the Act, an impeachment motion may originate in either House of Parliament. **To initiate** **proceedings,** at least **100 members** of the **Lok Sabha** give a signed notice to the Speaker, or at least **50** **members** of the **Rajya Sabha** give a signed notice to the Chairman.
o In case the notices of motion are given on the same day in both the Houses, the Committee will be constituted only if the motion has been admitted in both the Houses and thereupon jointly by the Chairman and the Speaker. In case notices of motion are given in both the Houses on different dates, the notice that is given later shall stand rejected. o **If the motion is admitted**, **the Speaker or Chairman** (who receives it) will constitute a threemember committee to investigate the complaint. It will comprise: (i) a Supreme Court judge; (ii)
Chief Justice of a High Court; and (iii) a distinguished jurist.  The committee will frame charges based on which the investigation will be conducted. **So, statement II is not correct.**
● After concluding its investigation, the Committee will submit its report to the Speaker or Chairman, who will then lay the report before the relevant House of Parliament. If the report records a finding of misbehaviour or incapacity, the motion for removal will be taken up for consideration and debated.
o The motion for removal is required to be adopted by each House of Parliament by Special Majority, that is: (i) **a majority of the total membership of that House**; and (ii) **a majority of at least two-thirds of the members of that House present and voting**. o If the motion is adopted by this majority, the motion will be sent to the other House for adoption. Once the motion is adopted in both Houses, it is sent to the President, who will issue an order for the removal of the judge. **So, statement III is correct.** **Therefore, option (a) is the correct answer.**
**Knowledge Box**
Judges Removed or Impeached?
● The term ‘impeachment’ is not used in the Constitution which instead uses the term ‘removal’ in the case of judges.
● The term ‘impeachment’ is used only in the context of the removal of the President of India from office (Article 61).
**Relevance**: The Supreme Court rejected Justice Yashwant Varma’s challenge to the Lok Sabha Speaker setting up a committee to inquire into a motion for his removal.

questions.subject: Polity
questions.sectionGroup: Part III, IV & IVA: Rights and Duties
questions.questionText:
With reference to Fundamental Rights, consider
the following pairs:
Provision Article
I. - Protection against selfincrimination
Article 22
II. - Protection against arbitrary arrest and detention
Article 23
III. - Right to a clean and healthy environment
Article 21
IV. - Right to speedy trial Article 20
How many of the pairs given above are correctly
matched?
questions.explanationMarkdown:
**Answer: a** **Explanation**:
● **Article 20 of the Constitution of India** grants protection against arbitrary and excessive punishment to an accused person, whether a citizen or foreigner or legal person like a company or a corporation. **It** **contains three provisions in that direction**:
o **No ex-post-facto law**: No person shall be (i) convicted of any offence except for violation of a law in force at the time of the commission of the act, nor (ii) subjected to a penalty greater than that prescribed by the law in force at the time of the commission of the act.
▪ An ex-post-facto law is a law which imposes penalties retroactively, that is, upon acts already done, or which increases the penalty for the past acts. o **No double jeopardy**: No person shall be prosecuted and punished for the same offence more than once. o **No self-incrimination**: No person accused of any offence shall be compelled to be a witness against himself/herself (Under article 20(3)). **So, pair I is not correctly matched.**
● **Article 22 of the Constitution grants protection to persons who are arrested or detained.** Detention is of two types, namely, punitive and preventive. Punitive detention is to punish a person for an offence committed by him/her after trial and conviction in a court. Preventive detention, on the other hand, means detention of a person without trial and conviction by a court. **So, pair II is not correctly matched.**
● The **Right to live in a healthy environment as part of Article 21** of the Constitution of India was first recognised in Rural Litigation and Entitlement Kendra versus State of U.P., 1985.  In 1987, the Supreme
Court in M.C. Mehta versus Union of India treated the right to live in a pollution free environment as part of the fundamental right to life under Article 21 of the Constitution.
o In **M K Ranjitsinh & Ors versus Union of India case** the **Supreme Court of India** has held that the people have a right to be free from the adverse effects of climate change within the ambit of fundamental rights **under Articles 14 and 21** of the Constitution of India. **So, pair III is correctly** **matched.**
● The **Right to a Speedy Trial** is recognised by the Supreme Court under **Article 21** of the Constitution of India in The Hussainara Khatoon vs. Home Secretary, State of Bihar (1979). But the Court has not set a maximum time limit within which a criminal trial must be completed. **So, pair IV is not correctly** **matched.** **So, only one of the pairs given above is correctly matched** **Therefore, option (a) is the correct answer.** **Relevance**: Eminent members of society expresses their views in the  Delhi Riots cases.

questions.subject: Economy
questions.questionText:
Consider the following in respect of inflation and
the bond market:
I. 	 A rise in inflation
II. 	 Increased market borrowing by State
Governments
III. 	Sale of government securities by the Reserve
Bank of India
Which of the above can lead to an increase in
bond yields?
questions.explanationMarkdown:
**Answer: d** **Explanation**:
● A **bond** is a **fixed-income investment** product where individuals lend money to a government or company at a specified interest rate for a **predetermined period**. Owners of bonds are debtholders, or creditors, of the issuer. **The yield-to-maturity (YTM)** is the total return anticipated on a bond if it is held until the end of its lifetime.
● A rise in **inflation** leads investors to demand higher yields to protect their "real" (inflation-adjusted) returns. **Persistent inflation** forces the RBI to keep interest rates high or tighten liquidity, which weakens the transmission of previous easing measures. If market yields remain high due to inflation, the benefits of a low repo rate do not fully reach the end-user (borrowers). **So, point I is correct.**
● **Increased market borrowing** by State Governments typically **leads to higher bond yields** due to an oversupply of government securities in the market. When governments borrow heavily, they consume a larger share of the limited loanable funds, reducing the capital available for the private sector and driving up interest rates, **which leads to crowding out**. **So, point II is correct.**
● The RBI utilizes **Open Market Operations (OMO) sales (Selling bonds by RBI)** to absorb excess liquidity, which generally increases bond yields as selling bonds lowers their price. Conversely, if the RBI wanted to decrease bond yields, it would buy bonds to inject cash and drive-up bond prices. **So, point III** **is correct.** **Therefore, option (d) is the correct answer.**
**Knowledge Box**
**Characteristics of Bonds**
● **Face value or Par Value:** The value of the bond at maturity and the reference amount the bond issuer uses when calculating interest payments.
● **Coupon Rate:** The rate of interest the bond issuer will pay on the face value of the bond, expressed as a percentage.
● **Coupon Dates:** The dates on which the bond issuer will make interest payments.
● **Maturity Date:** The date on which the bond will mature and the bond issuer will pay the bondholder the face value of the bond.
● **Issue Price:** The price at which the bond issuer originally sells the bonds. In many cases, bonds are issued at par.
**Relevance**: Reserve Bank of India is worried about heavy State debt supply may distort bond yield curve.

questions.subject: Geography
questions.microTopic: Critical and Strategic Minerals (Lithium, Cobalt, Titanium, KABIL, REEs, etc.)
questions.questionText:
Consider the following statements:
**Statement I:**
Most rare earth element mines globally are
developed as open-pit mines.
**Statement II:**
Rare earth bearing minerals are widely dispersed
and often associated with radioactive elements,
requiring bulk mining and careful waste
management.
Which one of the following is correct in respect of
the above statements?

options.a:
Both Statement I and Statement II are correct
and Statement II explains Statement I
options.b:
Both Statement I and Statement II are correct
but Statement II does not explain Statement I
options.c:
Statement I is correct but Statement II is not
correct
options.d:
Statement I is not correct but Statement II is
correct

questions.explanationMarkdown:
**Answer: a** **Explanation**:
● **Rare Earth Elements (REEs)** are a group of **17 chemically similar metallic elements comprising the** **15 lanthanides along with scandium and yttrium.** Despite their name, they are **relatively abundant in** **the Earth’s crust**; however, they are rarely found in economically viable concentrations. REEs are critical for modern technologies such as electronics, renewable energy systems, electric vehicles, and defence applications.
● Most **rare earth elements (REEs)** are not found in nature in their pure form; rather, they **occur within** **specific minerals** **that serve as their ores.** Although REEs are relatively abundant in the Earth’s crust, they are seldom concentrated in high-grade deposits. The minerals containing them are **typically** **distributed in low concentrations over large volumes of rock and are often located in near-surface** **formations.** Because of this dispersed and low-grade nature, bulk extraction becomes necessary, **making** **open-pit mining the most economically viable metho**d for their recovery. **So, statement I is correct.**
● The principal **rare earth bearing minerals**, such as **monazite and bastnäsite**, are widely distributed and frequently occur in association with radioactive elements like thorium and uranium. Their low concentration requires the excavation and processing of substantial quantities of ore and overburden. Additionally, the presence of radioactive elements necessitates careful handling and disposal of tailings to prevent environmental contamination. These **geological and compositional characteristics of rare** **earth bearing minerals explain why open-pit mining predominates in rare earth extraction. So,** **statement II is correct.** **So, both Statement–I and Statement–II are correct and Statement-II explains Statement–I.** **Therefore, option (a) is the correct answer.** **Relevance**: Public sector miner Coal India also acquired a block having REEs in Ramtek near Nagpur.

questions.subject: History
questions.questionText:
With reference to of tribal culture in India,
consider the following information:
Fair Associated Tribe
I. - Samakka Saralamma Koya
II. - Tusu Parab Santhal
III. - Si-Donyi Kondh
IV. - Boda Tyohar Hatti tribe
How many of the pairs given above are correctly
matched?
questions.explanationMarkdown:
**Answer: c** **Explanation**:
● **Sammakka Saralamma Jatara or Medaram Jatara** is a festival of honouring the goddesses celebrated in the state of Telangana. The Jatara begins at Medaram in Tadvai mandal in Jayashankar Bhupalpally district. The rituals are conducted by **Koya tribe priests** in accordance with Koya customs and traditions.
o Medaram is a remote place in the Eturnagaram Wildlife Sanctuary, a part of Dandakaranya, the largest surviving forest belt in the Deccan. Jatara is celebrated during the time the goddesses of the tribals are believed to visit them. It is celebrated to honor the mother-daughter duo who fought the Kakatiya dynasty's tax collection during a severe drought. **So, row I is correctly matched.**
● **Tusu Parab** is a vibrant, month-long harvest festival celebrated by tribal communities (Santhal, Kurmi) in Jharkhand, West Bengal, and Odisha, culminating on Makar Sankranti (mid-January). It **honors** **Goddess Tusu, symbolizing fertility and prosperity**. **So, row II is correctly matched.**
● **Si-Donyi is a socio-religious festival of the Tagin tribe in Arunachal Pradesh**. It marks the Tagin New Year. The community prays to Si" (the Earth) and "Donyi" (the Sun) supreme deities for universal peace, prosperity, and a bountiful harvest. **So, row III is not correctly matched.**
● **Boda Tyohar**, also known as **Magho ka Tyohar**, is the largest month-long annual festival of the **Hatti** **tribe**, celebrated in parts of **Himachal Pradesh and Uttarakhand**. The Hatti community was granted Scheduled Tribe status by Parliament on 4 August 2023. The **festival symbolises the central role of** **women in Hatti society**, as brothers visit their married sisters with traditional gifts known as Saje ka Duna, while sisters return to their parental homes to celebrate with their families, thereby strengthening familial bonds and kinship ties.  **So, row IV is correctly matched.** **So, in only three of the above rows, the information is correctly matched.** **Therefore, option (c) is the correct answer.** **Relevance**: Boda Tyohar, the biggest annual festival of the Hati tribe in Himachal Pradesh's Sirmour is celebrated by over three lakh community members.

questions.subject: Science & Technology
questions.questionText:
Consider the following statements:
**Statement I:**
The net gravitational force becomes exactly
zero at certain locations in outer space where no
massive bodies are present.
**Statement II:**
Gravity is the weakest of the four fundamental
forces.
Which one of the following is correct in respect of
the above statements?

options.a:
Both Statement I and Statement II are correct
and Statement II explains Statement I
options.b:
Both Statement I and Statement II are correct
but Statement II does not explain Statement I
options.c:
Statement I is correct but Statement II is not
correct
options.d:
Statement I is not correct but Statement II is
correct

questions.explanationMarkdown:
**Answer: d** **Explanation**:
● **Gravitational force has an infinite range**, and every mass in the universe exerts some gravitational influence, however small. Therefore, **there are no regions in outer space where the net gravitational** **force becomes zero merely because no massive bodies are present.** A zero net gravitational force can occur only **at specific equilibrium points** where the gravitational pulls of two or more massive bodies balance each other, **not due to the absence of mass. So, statement I is not correct.**
● Among the **four fundamental forces of nature**—**strong nuclear, electromagnetic, weak nuclear, and** **gravitational**—**gravity is the weakest** in terms of intrinsic strength. However, the relative weakness of gravity does not imply its absence in empty space. Even though gravity is weak compared to the other fundamental forces, **it still acts over infinite distances** **and never reduces to zero merely due to** **isolation from nearby masses.** **So, statement II is correct.** **So, Statement-I is incorrect, but statement-II is correct.** **Therefore, option (d) is the correct answer.** **Relevance**: ISRO announces the Indian Microgravity Experiments - IMEx-2026, an Announcement of Opportunity inviting proposals from the research community to develop and demonstrate scientifically meaningful microgravity experiments.

questions.subject: Polity
questions.questionText:
With reference to the Financial Intelligence
Unit–India (FIU-IND), consider the following
statements:
I. 	 It is headed by the Union Finance Minister of
India.
II. 	 It is responsible for coordinating with national
and international agencies against money
laundering and financing of terrorism.
III. 	It is the nodal body responsible for enforcing
tax laws related to virtual digital assets in
India.
IV. 	 The Prevention of Money Laundering Act
(PMLA), 2002, mandates financial entities to
furnish information of suspicious transactions
to FIU-IND.
Which of the statements given above are correct?
questions.explanationMarkdown:
**Answer**: **d** **Explanation**:
● To align with international standards set by the Financial Action Task Force (FATF), the **Financial** **Intelligence Unit – India (FIU-IND)** was set up by the Government of India in 2004. FIU-IND **operates** **under the Department of Revenue, Ministry of Finance.**
o **It is headed by a director appointed by the Government of India**. It reports directly to the Economic Intelligence Council (EIC), which is presided over by the Finance Minister. **So,** **statement I is not correct.**
● It is tasked with collecting and analyzing information about suspicious financial transactions to prevent money laundering, terror financing, and other financial crimes. FIU-IND is also responsible for **coordinating and strengthening efforts** of **national and international intelligence, investigation and** **enforcement agencies in pursuing the global efforts against money laundering and financing of** **terrorism.** **So, statement II is correct.**
● **FIU-IND regulates Virtual Digital Asset (VDA)** service providers **for anti-money laundering (AML)** **compliance**; it is **not responsible for enforcing tax laws.** As per Indian regulatory guidelines, cryptocurrency exchanges are required to conduct due diligence and complete KYC (Know Your Customer) procedures. This includes collecting details such as the customer’s occupation and income range, a selfie with liveness detection, geolocation data with timestamp and IP address, and verifying the bank account through the penny drop method. **So, statement III is not correct**
● **Section 12 of the Prevention of Money Laundering Act (PMLA), 2002**, mandates that banking companies, financial institutions, and intermediaries (reporting entities) maintain records of transactions, verify client identities, and **furnish information to the Financial Intelligence Unit-India (FIU-IND).** **Records must be kept for five years**, covering both executed and attempted transactions. **So, statement** **IV is correct.** **Therefore, option (d) is the correct answer.** **Relevance**: Financial Intelligence Unit (FIU-IND) updated the existing ‘AML & CFT Guidelines for Reporting Entities Providing Services Related to Virtual Digital Assets.

questions.subject: History
questions.questionText:
Consider the following pairs:
Personality Literary Contribution
I. - Narayana Guru Amar Pratinidhi
II. - Srimanta Sankardev Kirtan Ghosha
III. - Saint Thiruvalluvar Thirukkural
IV. - Bhupen Hazarika Atmopadesa Satakam
How many of the pairs given above are correctly matched?
questions.explanationMarkdown:
**Answer: b** **Explanation**:
● **Narayana Guru** (August 20, 1854 to September 20, 1928, Kerala) was a social reformer, poet, and Hindu sage who led a movement against the Hindu caste system for ezahava community. He believed that all people are equal and thus belong to just one caste, the caste of humankind. He expressed this idea in his famous saying, “One caste, one religion, one god for humanity. He had written **Atmopadesa Satakam** (One Hundred Verses on Self-Instruction) in **Malayalam** in around 1897. **So, pair I is not correctly** **matched.**
● **Srimanta Sankardev was born in 1449** at Alipukhuri near Bordua of Nagaon district, Assam.  **Shankardev wrote his first work, Harish Chandra Upakhyan**, during his school days. He played a significant role in preaching to humanity and protecting the human religion that is **‘Ek Sarania** **Nam Dharma’** in praise of Lord Vishnu and he established Neo Vaishnavism in Assam.
o He had written **Kirtan Ghosha. Kirtan Ghosha** is a collection of 27 poetical works meant for community singing (Kirtan). It was composed primarily to propagate the Ekasarana Dharma (religion of seeking refuge in one God. S**o, pair II is correctly matched.** o He also translated a few portions of the Bhagavata Purana into Assamese to ensure that the common people could understand the scriptures.
● **Saint Thiruvalluvar was a Sangam-era saint**. He is regarded as an Avatara of Brahma. He had written the Thirukkural. **Thirukkural consists** of 1,330 couplets (Kurals) and is divided into three parts: Virtue (Aram), Wealth (Porul), and Love (Inbam). **It is often regarded as the fifth Veda. So, pair III is** **correctly matched.**
● **Bhupen Hazarika (8 September, 1926 -5 November, 2011)** was a cultural icon from Assam and Northeast India. His songs and lyrics were in Assamese, Bengali and Hindi. He was known as the bard of
the Brahmaputra. He is popularly known as the Bard of Brahmaputra  and Sudhakantho  (Pure Voice). He also **edited Amar Pratinidhi,** an **Assamese literary magazine**. He received the Bharat Ratna posthumously. He used his art to support marginalized communities and promote social reform. His famous **song “Manuhe Manuhar Babe**” (For Man) expresses humanistic values. **So, pair IV is not** **correctly matched.** **So, only three of the pairs given above are correctly matched.** **Therefore, option (b) is the correct answer.** **Relevance**: Social, religious and literary icons of states of West Bengal, Tamil Nadu, Assam and Kerala received special mention in the President's address to the joint sitting of both Houses of Parliament.

questions.subject: International Relations
questions.microTopic: Global Groupings and Agreements (FTA, Paris Agreement, NPT, QUAD, etc.)
questions.questionText:
What is the objective of the Palermo Protocol,
which has been in the news recently?

options.a:
Reduce carbon emissions through forest
conservation
options.b:
Regulation of international e-commerce and
digital taxation
options.c:
Prevent and address human trafficking, with
special focus on protecting women and children
options.d:
Protection of international trade routes in the
Mediterranean

questions.explanationMarkdown:
**Answer: c** **Explanation**:
● The **Palermo Protocol or UN Protocol to Prevent, Suppress and Punish Trafficking in Persons** is the world's primary legal instrument to combat human trafficking. The Protocol was adopted by the United Nations in November 2000 as part of the **United Nations Convention against Transnational Organized** **Crime (UNTOC)**.
● It is the **first legally binding instrument with an internationally recognized definition of human** **trafficking and it explicitly emphasizes protection of women and children, who are particularly** **vulnerable.** This definition provides a vital tool for the identification of victims, whether men, women or children, and for the detection of all forms of exploitation which constitute human trafficking.
o Countries that ratify this treaty **must criminalize human trafficking and develop anti-** **trafficking laws in line with the Protocol’s legal provisions.** They must provide protection and assistance to victims of human trafficking and ensure that their rights are fully respected.
● **It has three supplementary protocols:** o Protocol to Prevent, Suppress and Punish Trafficking in Persons, Especially Women and Children. o Protocol Against the Smuggling of Migrants by Land, Sea and Air. o Protocol Against the Illicit Manufacturing of and Trafficking in Firearms.
● **India ratified the UNTOC and the Palermo Protocol in May 2011.** Its provisions are now mirrored in modern Indian laws, such as Section 143 of the Bharatiya Nyaya Sanhita (BNS), 2023. **Therefore, option (c) is the correct answer.** **Relevance**: The Supreme Court in its recent decision in K. P. Kiran Kumar versus State has held that trafficking grossly violates children’s fundamental right to life as guaranteed by the Constitution.

questions.subject: International Relations
questions.microTopic: International Organizations (UN, IMF, World Bank, ASEAN, BRICS, etc.)
questions.questionText:
Consider the following countries:
I. 	 Ireland
II. 	 Iceland
III. 	Liechtenstein
IV. 	 Luxembourg
V. 	 Norway
VI. 	Finland
How many of the above are part of the European Union?
questions.explanationMarkdown:
**Answer: a** **Explanation**:
● The **European Union (EU) is a political and economic bloc of 27 European countries** that functions as a single market with common trade rules, regulatory standards, and coordinated policies in several sectors. **India has recently concluded a major trade agreement with the EU** aimed at enhancing market access, boosting bilateral trade, promoting investment flows, and strengthening cooperation in areas such as technology, sustainability, and supply chains. **This agreement reflects deepening India–EU strategic** **and economic ties.**
● The **European Free Trade Association (EFTA)** is an intergovernmental organization established in 1960 to promote free trade and economic integration for **four** **member states: Iceland, Liechtenstein, Norway, and** **Switzerland.**
● **Switzerland, Iceland, Norway and Liechtenstein are not** **members of the European Union.**
o However, **Ireland, Luxembourg and Finland are** **part of the EU bloc.**
● The **India-EFTA Trade and Economic Partnership** **Agreement (TEPA)** officially came into force on October 1, 2025.
● The deal brings together the second- and fourth-largest single customs blocs, with the Indian government estimating the combined market size to be about $24 trillion. India has signed about eight FTAs in the last four years. While the other eight FTAs together accounted for about 16% of India’s total trade in 2024-25, in the latest full year of data available, the EU itself accounted for nearly 12%. **So, only three of the above countries are part of the European Union.** **Therefore, option (a) is the correct answer.** **Relevance**: Negotiations on the India-European Union Free Trade Agreement (FTA) dubbed the ‘mother of all deals’ by leaders on both sides, officially concluded on January 27, 2026.

questions.subject: International Relations
questions.microTopic: Global Groupings and Agreements (FTA, Paris Agreement, NPT, QUAD, etc.)
questions.questionText:
Consider the following International Conventions:
I. 	 International Convention on the Protection
of the Rights of All Migrant Workers and
Members of Their Families
II. 	 International Labour Organisation (ILO)
Migration for Employment Convention
III. 	International Labour Organisation (ILO)
Migrant Workers Convention
IV. 	 Global Compact for Safe, Orderly and
Regular Migration
V. 	 Protocol against the Smuggling of Migrants
by Land, Sea and Air
India is **not** a party to how many of the above?
questions.explanationMarkdown:
**Answer: b** **Explanation**:
● **International Convention on the Protection of the Rights of All Migrant Workers and Members of** **Their Families (1990)** is a comprehensive human rights treaty aimed at protecting the rights of migrant workers, whether they are in a regular (legal) or irregular (undocumented) situation. **Its four key pillars** **are:** Non-discrimination (Article 2), Best interest of the child (Article 3), Right to life, survival and development (Article 6), Right to be heard (Article 12). But **India has neither signed nor ratified this** **UN treaty**. **So, point I is correct.**
● The protection of migrant workers lies at the heart of the ILO’s mandate, as articulated in its Constitution,
which calls for the protection of “the interests of workers when employed in countries other than their own” (ILO Constitution, 1919, Preamble). The **ILO has developed two dedicated conventions to** **regulate labour migration, safeguard the rights of migrant workers**, and promote non-discrimination and equality of treatment:
o **Migration for Employment Convention (Revised), 1949** (No. 97): its aim to regulate the conditions under which labour migration occurs, including recruitment. It requires States to ensure equal treatment—without discrimination based on nationality, race, religion, or sex—between nationals and regularly employed migrant workers.  **India is not a party of this convention. So,** **point II is correct.**
o **Migrant Workers (Supplementary Provisions) Convention, 1975 (No. 143):** It requires States to take action against abusive forms of labour migration while upholding the basic human rights of all migrant workers. **India is not a party of this convention. So, point III is correct.**
● **The Global Compact for Safe, Orderly and Regular Migration** is a non-legally binding framework. It is the first intergovernmental negotiated, non-binding UN agreement (adopted 2018) covering all dimensions of international migration. **India is a party to this convention**. **So, point IV is not correct.**
● The **Protocol Against the Smuggling of Migrants by Land, Sea and Air,** supplements the Convention against Transnational Organised Crime, was adopted by the United Nations General Assembly in 2000. It is also referred to as the Smuggling Protocol. The Smuggling Protocol entered into force on 28 January 2004. **India is a signatory to this convention. So, point V is not correct.** **So, only three of the above given conventions/protocols are not signed by India.** **Therefore, option (b) is the correct answer.** **Relevance**: The police detained 103 Bangladeshi nationals from eight districts in West Bengal in 2025.

questions.subject: History
questions.questionText:
With reference to Ponduru Khadi, consider the
following statements:
I. 	 It is a handspun and handwoven cotton fabric
produced from short-staple hill cotton that
is naturally pest-resistant and grown using
chemical-free farming.
II. 	 Cotton ginning for making it is performed
using a jawbone of the indigenous Valuga
fish.
III. 	Recently Tamil Nadu was awarded the
Geographical Indication (GI) tag for it.
Which of the statements given above are correct?
questions.explanationMarkdown:
**Ponduru Khadi** is a rare, traditional handwoven cotton fabric from **Andhra Pradesh**, known for its fine quality and artisanal craftsmanship. It reflects centuries-old skills, especially in spinning and weaving using eco-friendly methods. The fabric represents the rich cultural heritage and Gandhian Swadeshi ideals.
● Ponduru Khadi is **made from hill cotton varieties like Punasa and Red cotton.** These are **naturally** **resistant to pests** and cultivated **without chemical inputs**, making the fabric eco-friendly and sustainable. **So, statement I is correct.**
● One of the unique features of Ponduru Khadi is **cleaning and ginning the cotton manually using the** **jawbone of the Valuga fish**, a traditional technique not found elsewhere. This preserves the quality of fibers. **So, statement II is correct.**
● Ponduru Khadi was awarded the **GI tag for Andhra Pradesh**, not Tamil Nadu. The GI recognition safeguards the authenticity of the craft and legally protects its identity. **So, statement III is not correct.**
**Knowledge Box:**
● Location: Ponduru village, Srikakulam district, Andhra Pradesh
● Other Names: Patnulu
● Yarn Count: Exceptionally high, around 100–120
● Significance: Represents Gandhian Swadeshi ideology and Aatmanirbhar Bharat initiatives
**Therefore, option (a) is the correct answer.**
**Relevance:** Ponduru Khadi of Andhra Pradesh has been granted the Geographical Indication (GI) tag, with the registration awarded in favour of the Khadi and Village Industries Commission (KVIC) by the Geographical Indications Registry.

questions.subject: Environment
questions.questionText:
Consider the following statements with regard
to the Solid Waste Management (SWM) Rules, 2026:
I. 	 They mandate four-way segregation of waste
into wet, dry, sanitary, and special care waste.
II. 	 Urban Local Bodies (ULBs) must ensure
segregation and material recovery before adopting waste-to-energy methods.
III. 	Informal waste workers are guaranteed social
security and livelihood provisions.
How many of the above statements are **not** correct?
questions.explanationMarkdown:
**The Solid Waste Management (SWM) Rules, 2026** represent India’s attempt to strengthen waste governance and ensure stricter compliance compared to the 2016 Rules. These rules aim to make segregation at source,
material recovery, and environmentally sound waste processing more enforceable, while also recognising informal waste workers.
● The 2026 Rules mandate **four-way segregation of waste wet, dry, sanitary, and special care waste**. This is an upgrade from the 2016 two-way segregation (wet and dry) and reflects a sharper focus on health, environmental safety, and proper management of hazardous and sanitary waste. **So, statement I is** **correct.**
● Although the Rules emphasise segregation and recovery, there is **no explicit requirement for ULBs** to **demonstrate minimum segregation or recovery levels before employing waste-to-energy** (WtE) technology. WtE is treated as a permissible downstream option rather than being conditional on upstream compliance. **So, statement II is not correct.**
● Informal waste workers are acknowledged in the system, but the guaranteed social security and livelihood provisions remain aspirational rather than operationally mandated. The rules focus on recognition and inclusion, not enforcement of labor rights or benefits. **So, statement III is not correct.** **Therefore, option (b) is the correct answer.**
**Relevance:** Almost a decade after India notified the Solid Waste Management (SWM) Rules, 2016, the Union government has unveiled the new regulatory framework: the SWM Rules, 2026.

questions.subject: Science & Technology
questions.questionText:
Bacillus subtilis, a probiotic bacterium found in
the environment, the human gut, and fermented foods that aids digestion and nutrient absorption, was recently designated as a State microbe.
Which Indian state became the first to officially declare a State bacterium by conferring this status on Bacillus subtilis?
questions.explanationMarkdown:
● **Bacillus subtilis Characteristics:** ○ It is a probiotic, “good” bacteria widely present in soil, water, fermented foods, and the human gut. ○ Supports digestion, produces essential enzymes, and aids in nutrient absorption and immune modulation. ○ Known for its spore-forming ability, making it resilient in harsh environmental conditions and suitable for industrial and laboratory applications.
● **Kerala’s Initiative:** ○ Chief Minister Pinarayi Vijayan declared B. subtilis as the State microbe in January 2026, making Kerala the first Indian state to have an official State microbe. ○ The government also dedicated the Centre of Excellence in Microbiome (CoEM) to study microbes’ role in health and environmental sustainability. **Therefore, option (b) is the correct answer.**
**Relevance:** Kerala the first State in the country to officially declare its own State microbe. Chief Minister dedicated to the people Centre of Excellence in Microbiome, which will scientifically study the role of microorganisms in health and environment.

questions.subject: Environment
questions.questionText:
With reference to the report ‘Global Water
Bankruptcy’, consider the following statements:
I. 	 It was released by the World Bank in
collaboration with the Food and Agriculture Organization.
II. 	 About 70% of global freshwater withdrawals
are used for industrial and domestic purposes.
III. 	According to it, droughts are increasingly
anthropogenic, driven by land degradation, groundwater depletion, and unsustainable water management.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
questions.explanationMarkdown:
**The Global Water Bankruptcy report** (2026) by UNU-INWEH highlights that global freshwater use has exceeded sustainable limits. Agriculture is the main consumer of water, causing ecosystem degradation and food insecurity. The report also notes that human activities increasingly drive droughts.
● The report was published by the **United Nations University Institute for Water, Environment and** **Health (UNU-INWEH),** not the World Bank or FAO. It focuses on scientific assessment of water stress
rather than policy prescriptions. Misattributing the publisher changes the understanding of its scope. **So,** **statement I is not correct.**
● **According to the report roughly 70% of freshwater withdrawals are for agriculture,** not industrial or domestic use. Industry and domestic sectors account for a smaller proportion. The report stresses that unsustainable agricultural water use is the key driver of water scarcity. **So, statement II is not correct.**
● According to the report droughts are now amplified by human activities, including land mismanagement, over-irrigation, and excessive groundwater extraction. This makes droughts more frequent and severe than climate-only driven events. It has major implications for food security and livelihoods. **So, statement III** **is correct.** **Therefore, option (a) is the correct answer.**
**Relevance:** The latest United Nations report makes clear that in many regions, agriculture is no longer operating in a temporary water crisis but within a state of global water bankruptcy.

questions.subject: Economy
questions.questionText:
With reference to the 'NPS Swasthya Pension
Scheme', consider the following statements:
I. 	 It is a mandatory contributory pension scheme
launched by Pension Fund Regulatory and Development Authority (PFRDA) for all government employees.
II. 	 Subscribers above 40 years of age may
transfer a portion of their contributions from
their regular NPS account to this scheme.
III. 	Partial withdrawals for medical purposes are
allowed only after completing three years
from the date of joining.
IV. 	 In cases of inpatient medical treatment, a
subscriber may opt for premature exit with
full lump-sum withdrawal if medical expenses
exceed 70% of the corpus.
Which of the statements given above are correct?
questions.explanationMarkdown:
● The **NPS Swasthya Pension Scheme** is a recently introduced **initiative under the Pension Fund** **Regulatory and Development Authority (PFRDA)** **within the framework of the National Pension** **System (NPS)**. The scheme is designed to help subscribers create a dedicated corpus specifically for meeting healthcare and medical expenses, particularly during retirement years. It functions as an optional health-focused component linked to the existing NPS structure.
● The scheme is **not mandatory** and is **not restricted to government employees**. It is a voluntary facility available to eligible NPS subscribers who choose to allocate part of their retirement savings toward healthcare needs. **So, statement I is not correct.**
● Subscribers above 40 years of age **are permitted to transfer a portion of their accumulated corpus** **from their regular NPS** account into the Swasthya Pension Scheme account, subject to prescribed limits and conditions. **So, statement II is correct.**
● The scheme does not prescribe a mandatory three-year lock-in period for partial withdrawals. Instead, partial withdrawals for medical purposes are permitted once the subscriber has accumulated a **minimum** **corpus of ₹50,000** in the Swasthya Pension Scheme account. Withdrawals can be made for outpatient or inpatient medical treatment through the designated health benefit administrator, subject to the available balance in the account. Therefore, eligibility for withdrawal is linked to the **minimum corpus threshold**, **not to completion of three years** from the date of joining. **So, statement III is not correct.**
● In cases of **inpatient medical treatment where expenses in a single instance exceed 70% of the total** **corpus** available in the Swasthya Pension Scheme account, the **subscriber may opt for premature exit** **with 100% lump-sum withdrawal.** Any surplus amount remaining after settling the medical expenses is transferred back to the subscriber’s regular NPS account. **So, statement IV is correct.**
**Knowledge Box:**
● Launched by: PFRDA (Pension Fund Regulatory and Development Authority)
● Target Audience: Existing NPS subscribers, especially above 40 years of age
● Objective: Facilitate health expense management through pension savings
● Withdrawal Rules: Partial withdrawals post 3 years, full exit for critical medical cases
● Relevance: Combines social security and healthcare financing for older adults
**Therefore, option (c) is the correct answer.**
**Relevance:** The Pension Fund Regulatory and Development Authority (‘PFRDA) has launched NPS Swasthya Pension Scheme which will provide financial support for out-patient and in-patient medical expenses of the subscribers.

questions.subject: Science & Technology
questions.microTopic: Information and Communication Technology, Robotics, AI
questions.questionText:
Consider the following:
I. 	 Precision agriculture to monitor soil moisture
and optimise irrigation
II. 	 Notifying friends and family automatically in
case of a car crash
III. 	Measuring air quality and radiation levels in
urban areas
IV. 	 Reducing energy consumption in smart
buildings
How many of the above are applications of the
Internet of Things (IoT)?
questions.explanationMarkdown:
● The **Internet of Things (IoT)** refers to a network of physical devices embedded with sensors, software and connectivity that enable them to **collect, exchange and act on data**. It includes both communication between devices and interaction with cloud-based platforms.
● The availability of **low-cost microchips** and **high-bandwidth telecommunications** has enabled billions of devices to be connected to the internet. As a result, everyday objects, such as **toothbrushes, vacuum** **cleaners, vehicles and industrial machines,** can sense their environment, transmit data and respond intelligently to user needs. The following are the applications of the **Internet of Things (IoT):**
● **IoT-based irrigation is a key component of precision agriculture**, enabling the **precise application of** **water through real-time monitoring** of soil moisture, temperature, and crop requirements using interconnected sensors. Unlike conventional uniform irrigation methods, it dynamically regulates the timing and quantity of water supplied based on crop type, soil characteristics, and environmental variables such as slope, sunlight exposure, and evapotranspiration rates. This data-driven, site-specific approach optimizes water-use efficiency, minimizes wastage, and enhances overall crop productivity and sustainability.  **So, point I is correct.**
● **Connected cars** are vehicles linked to the internet through systems such as smart dashcams, infotainment units, or onboard gateways. They collect data from components like brakes, accelerator, speedometer, wheels and fuel systems to assess driver behaviour and vehicle health. Their applications include fleet monitoring, tracking driving behaviour, **automatic crash alerts** and predictive vehicle maintenance. **So,** **point II is correct.**
● IoT applications have made urban planning and infrastructure maintenance more efficient. IoT applications can be used for:
○ **Measuring air quality and radiation levels. So, point III is correct.** ○ Reducing energy bills with smart lighting systems. ○ Detecting maintenance needs for critical infrastructures such as streets, bridges, and pipelines. ○ Increasing profits through efficient parking management.
● Buildings such as college campuses and commercial buildings use IoT applications to drive greater operational efficiencies. IoT devices can be used in **smart buildings** for:
○ **Reducing energy consumption. So, point IV is correct.** ○ Lowering maintenance costs. ○ Utilizing work spaces more efficiently. **So, all four of the above can be considered as applications of the Internet of Things (IoT).** **Therefore, option (d) is the correct answer.**

questions.subject: Environment
questions.microTopic: Biodiversity (IUCN, WWF, CBD, Cartagena, Nagoya, etc.)
questions.questionText:
Consider the following statements with regard to
the High Seas Treaty:
I. 	 It is the first legally binding global treaty
providing a framework for creating Marine
Protected Areas (MPAs) in international
waters.
II. 	 It provides for mandatory environmental
impact assessments for potentially harmful
activities.
Which of the statements given above is/are
correct?
questions.explanationMarkdown:
● The **High Seas Treaty**, formally known as the Agreement under the United Nations Convention on the Law of the Sea on the conservation and sustainable use of marine biological diversity of areas beyond national jurisdiction **(BBNJ Agreement)**, marks a historic step in global ocean governance. It is the **first** **legally binding international instrument aimed specifically at conserving marine biodiversity in** **areas beyond national jurisdiction**, which constitute nearly two-thirds of the world’s oceans. The treaty addresses growing concerns such as overfishing, deep-sea mining, marine pollution, and climate change impacts on the high seas.
● The treaty **establishes the first comprehensive and legally binding global framework for the creation** **of Marine Protected Areas (MPAs) in international waters.** Until now, protection of high seas biodiversity was fragmented and sectoral, with no overarching mechanism to designate and manage MPAs in areas beyond national jurisdiction. The treaty fills this institutional gap and enables coordinated conservation efforts. **So, statement I is correct.**
● The treaty **mandates that countries ensure environmental impact assessments (EIAs)** are conducted for planned activities **that may have significant adverse effects on the marine environment of the high** **seas.** This provision strengthens accountability and promotes precautionary decision-making before undertaking **potentially harmful activities such as large-scale fishing, seabed mining, or marine** **genetic resource exploration. So, statement II is correct.** **Therefore, option (c) is the correct answer.**
**Relevance:** Recently, the UN High Seas Treaty officially entered into force on January 17, 2026.

questions.subject: Geography
questions.questionText:
Consider the following statements:
**Statement I:**
Auroras display multicoloured lights in the high-
latitude regions near the poles.
**Statement II:**
Excited charged particles interact with gases such
as oxygen and nitrogen in the upper atmosphere.
Which one of the following is correct in respect of
the above statements?

options.a:
Both Statement I and Statement II are correct
and Statement II explains Statement I
options.b:
Both Statement I and Statement II are correct
but Statement II does not explain Statement I
options.c:
Statement I is correct but Statement II is not
correct
options.d:
Statement I is not correct but Statement II is
correct

questions.explanationMarkdown:
● **Auroras** are natural light displays that appear as shimmering, curtain-like patterns in the night sky. They **exhibit a range of colours, blue, red, yellow, green, and orange,** which gently shift and change shape.
● Auroras are **visible only at night** and occur predominantly in the **high-latitude regions near the poles**. They are observed almost every night close to the Arctic and Antarctic Circles (around 66.5° north and south of the Equator). In the **Northern Hemisphere**, the phenomenon is known as the **aurora borealis** or Northern Lights, while in the **Southern Hemisphere** it is called the **aurora australis** or Southern Lights. **So, statement I is correct.**
● The different colours of auroras arise **when excited charged particles transfer energy to atmospheric** **gases such as oxygen and nitrogen.** For example, oxygen produces green and red light at different altitudes. Importantly, **nitrogen atoms contribute to the reddish and bluish hues often seen along the** **lower edges of auroral displays. Collisions involving hydrogen and helium can produce blue and** **purple light, though these wavelengths are generally faint and difficult for the human eye to detect.** Thus, the multicoloured appearance of auroras is directly explained by the interaction of energetic particles with different atmospheric gases. **So, statement II is correct.** **So, both Statement I and Statement II are correct and Statement II explains Statement I.** **Therefore, option (a) is the correct answer.**
**Relevance:** Recently, the red aurora appeared over Ladakh, in northern India’s high-altitude region.

questions.subject: Environment
questions.microTopic: Pollution Related (E-Waste, Solid Waste, Pollutants, etc.)
questions.questionText:
With reference to the Basel Convention, consider
the following statements:
I. 	 It promotes the environmentally sound
management of hazardous wastes.
II. 	 Its provisions do not cover wastes generated
from military activities.
III. 	It explicitly excludes radioactive wastes that
are regulated under separate international
control systems.
Which of the statements given above are correct?
questions.explanationMarkdown:
● The **Basel Convention on the Control of Transboundary Movements of Hazardous Wastes and their** **Disposal** was adopted in **1989** and it came into force in **1992**. It is the most comprehensive global environmental agreement on **hazardous wastes and other wastes.** The Convention covers toxic, poisonous, explosive, corrosive, flammable, ecotoxic and infectious wastes. Parties also have an obligation to minimise the quantities that are transported, to treat and dispose of wastes as close as possible to their place of generation and to prevent or minimise the generation of wastes at source.
● The **Basel Convention** governs the **transboundary movement of hazardous and other wastes** and requires its Parties to ensure their **environmentally sound management and disposal** to safeguard human health and the environment. **So, statement I is correct.**
● The Basel Convention mainly regulates the transboundary movement of hazardous industrial and household wastes and its scope is **not limited solely to civilian waste, nor does it categorically exclude** **all military-generated waste.** Additionally, wastes arising from the normal operations of ships, whose discharge is governed by other international instruments, are excluded from the Convention’s scope. **So,** **statement II is not correct.**
● Under this Convention, wastes falling within any category listed in **Annex II** and subject to transboundary movement are classified as **“other wastes.”** However, wastes that are **radioactive in nature** and are governed by separate international control regimes or instruments specifically applicable to radioactive materials are **excluded from the scope of this Convention. So, statement III is correct.** **Therefore, option (c) is the correct answer.**

questions.subject: Economy
questions.questionText:
Consider the following information:
Suppose the Government's capital expenditure is
₹50,000 crore and its capital receipts (excluding
borrowings) are ₹15,000 crore. The Government’s
budget also shows a revenue deficit of ₹20,000
crore and interest payments of ₹8,000 crore.
Based on the above information, consider the
following statements:
I. 	 The government's capital deficit amounts to
₹35,000 crore.
II. 	 The government's fiscal deficit is ₹55,000
crore.
III. 	The government’s primary deficit is ₹47,000
crore.
Which of the statements given above is/are
correct?
questions.explanationMarkdown:
● **Capital expenditure** refers to government spending that leads to the **creation of permanent assets**, generates **periodic income**, or involves **loans and advances** to State governments and local bodies.
● **Capital receipts** are transactions that result in a **liability or a reduction in assets** of the government and are therefore **two-way in nature**. The funds received are expected to return to the government either as **regular income** or at the time of **disposal of the asset** created from such expenditure.
○ **Capital deficit** = Capital expenditure − Capital receipts (excluding borrowings) = 50,000 − 15,000 = **₹35,000 crore. So, statement I is correct.**
● **Fiscal Deficit:** It arises when the government’s total expenditure exceeds its total non-borrowed receipts, indicating the total borrowing requirement of the government.
● **Revenue Deficit:** It is the excess of revenue expenditure over revenue receipts and reflects the shortfall in meeting current (day-to-day) expenses from current income.
○ **Fiscal deficit** = Revenue deficit + Capital deficit = 20,000 + 35,000 = **₹55,000 crore. So, statement II is correct.**
● **Primary Deficit:** It is the fiscal deficit excluding interest payments and shows the extent of government borrowing used for purposes other than servicing past debt.
○ **Primary deficit** = Fiscal deficit − Interest payments = 55,000 − 8,000 = **₹47,000 crore. So, statement III is correct.** **Therefore, option (d) is the correct answer.**
**Relevance:** Essential budget terms that shape government spending, taxation, and economic policies ahead of India’s Union Budget 2026.

questions.subject: Economy
questions.questionText:
With reference to the Union Budget, consider the
following statements:
I. 	 The Appropriation Bill authorises the
Government to withdraw money from the Consolidated Fund of India.
II. 	 No amendment can be proposed to the
Appropriation Bill that changes the amount or destination of expenditure.
III. 	The Vote on Account allows the Government
to incur expenditure only on the revenue account and not on the capital account.
Which of the statements given above are correct?
questions.explanationMarkdown:
● An **Appropriation Bill** is a legislative proposal that authorizes the government to withdraw funds from the **Consolidated Fund of India** to meet its expenditure. It is introduced in the Lok Sabha after the annual budget is presented and approved.
● The **Constitution of India** mandates that **no money shall be withdrawn** from the Consolidated Fund of India **except under an appropriation made by law.** In compliance with this provision, an Appropriation Bill is introduced to authorize the withdrawal of funds from the Consolidated Fund of India to meet:
○ Grants voted by the Lok Sabha, and ○ Expenditure charged on the Consolidated Fund of India. **So, statement I is correct.**
● **The Lok Sabha discusses the bill, but no amendment may be moved** in either House of Parliament to the Appropriation Bill that seeks to **vary the amount or alter the destination of any grant voted by the** **Lok Sabha,** or to **modify the amount of expenditure** charged on the Consolidated Fund of India. **So,** **statement II is correct.**
● A **Vote on Account** is a temporary financial authorization granted by **Parliament** that permits the government to withdraw funds from the Consolidated Fund of India to meet essential expenditure for a limited period. A **Vote on Account** authorizes the government to withdraw funds from the Consolidated Fund of India to **meet estimated expenditure, both revenue and capital**. It is passed (or granted) after the general discussion on the budget is over. It is generally granted for two months for an amount equivalent to one-sixth of the total estimation. **So, statement III is not correct.** **Therefore, option (a) is the correct answer.**
**Relevance:** Recently, the Union Minister for Finance and Corporate Affairs presented the Union Budget 202627 in the Parliament.

questions.subject: Economy
questions.questionText:
Consider the following statements with regard to
Central Bank Digital Currencies (CBDCs):
I. 	 They are legal tender issued by a central bank
entirely in digital form.
II. 	 Transactions involving them must necessarily
pass through commercial bank accounts.
III. 	Their transactions cannot be deleted or
modified.
IV. 	 They can be designed to be programmable for
specific uses or conditions.
Which of the statements given above are correct?
questions.explanationMarkdown:
● **Central Bank Digital Currencies (CBDCs)** are digital representations of a nation’s fiat currency, issued and regulated by the **central bank,** with their value guaranteed by the government. Unlike cryptocurrencies, which are decentralized and prone to price volatility, **CBDCs are sovereign-backed** **instruments** designed to ensure stability, trust and monetary control.
● **CBDCs** are **legal tender issued by a central bank entirely in a digital form.** In India, the Reserve Bank of India has introduced the **e-rupee** on a limited scale. While it is equivalent in value to the physical rupee, the e-rupee exists independently as a digital store of value. It is held in a **dedicated digital wallet**, **separate from a bank account.** Unlike the **Unified Payments Interface (UPI)**, which enables the transfer of money between bank accounts, CBDC transactions **involve the direct transfer of value from** **one wallet to another,** with each transaction recorded on a **digital ledger (blockchain-based system)**. **So, statement I is correct and statement II is not correct.**
● Owing to their fully digital nature and integration with blockchain technology, CBDCs offer a **high degree** **of transparency**. A blockchain functions as a shared and immutable digital ledger, accessible to all authorized participants. Once a transaction is recorded, it becomes permanent and **cannot be deleted or** **modified;** any correction can only be effected through a subsequent **reverse transaction of equivalent** **value. So, statement III is correct.**
● CBDCs are **programmable by design,** allowing their use to be **restricted or conditioned.** As per the **Reserve Bank of India**, they can be programmed based on parameters such as **expiry dates, geographic** **boundaries, merchant registration codes, or specific merchant categories.** In principle, the scope of programmability is limited only by the objectives embedded in the code. **So, statement IV is correct.** **Therefore, option (d) is the correct answer.**
**Knowledge Box** **Other information related to Central Bank Digital Currencies (CBDCs):**
● CBDCs differ from private cryptocurrencies in that, where private cryptocurrencies like Bitcoin are decentralised by design, with no central issuer and no regulator, CBDCs have a very definite issuer and regulator: the central bank. Also, while most cryptocurrencies (apart from stablecoins) are not pegged to any real-world asset from which they can derive value, CBDCs are backed by the central bank and have a set value. That is, one e-rupee equals one rupee.
**Relevance:** The Reserve Bank of India has advised the Indian government to encourage BRICS countries to collaborate on using digital currencies for cross-border payments.

questions.subject: History
questions.questionText:
Consider the following statements with regard to
Padma Awards:
I. 	 It is announced every year on the eve of the
Republic Day.
II. 	 The Padma Awards Committee is headed by
the Prime Minister of India.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
questions.explanationMarkdown:
● The **Padma Awards** are one of the **highest civilian honours of India,** announced annually on the eve of **Republic Day.** The Awards are given in three categories: **Padma Vibhushan** (for exceptional and distinguished service), **Padma Bhushan** (distinguished service of higher order) and **Padma Shri** (distinguished service). The nomination process is open to the public. Even self-nomination can be made.
● The Padma Awards, instituted in **1954,** are announced **annually on the occasion of Republic Day,** except for brief interruptions during **1978–79 and 1993–97.** The awards are open to all persons without distinction of race, occupation, position, or gender. However, government servants, including employees of Public Sector Undertakings (PSUs), are generally not eligible, with an exception made for doctors and scientists. **So, statement I is correct.**
● All nominations for the Padma Awards are examined by the **Padma Awards Committee,** which is **constituted annually by the Prime Minister.** The Committee is chaired by the **Cabinet Secretary** and includes the Home Secretary, Secretary to the President and four to six eminent persons as members. Its recommendations are submitted to the Prime Minister and the President of India for final approval. **So,** **statement II is not correct.** **Therefore, option (a) is the correct answer.**
**Knowledge Box**
● The Padma Awards celebrate **distinguished and exceptional achievements** across all fields of activity. While the honours are typically reserved for living recipients, the Government may, in rare and highly deserving instances, confer them **posthumously.**
● To maintain the prestige of the hierarchy, a recipient can only be considered for a higher category of the Padma Award if at **least five years have passed** since their previous recognition.
● The awards are presented by the **President of India,** usually in the month of **March/April every year,** where the awardees are presented a Sanad (certificate) signed by the President and a medallion.
● The recipients are also given a **small replica of the medallion,** which they can wear during any ceremonial/State functions etc., if the awardees so desire. The names of the awardees are published in the Gazette of India on the day of the presentation ceremony.
● The total number of awards to be given in a year (excluding posthumous awards and to NRI/foreigners/OCIs) should not be **more than 120.**
● The award does not amount to a title and cannot be used as a **suffix or prefix** to the awardee’s name.
**Relevance:** Recently, the Government of India has announced 131 Padma awards for the year 2026.

questions.subject: Science & Technology
questions.questionText:
With reference to bio-bitumen technology,
consider the following statements:
I. 	 It involves the conversion of post-harvest rice
straw into bio-oil through pyrolysis.
II. 	 It can completely replace petroleum-based
bitumen in road construction.
III. 	The biomass pyrolysis process employed in
this technology produces gaseous fuels and high-grade carbon, which can be used as industrial inputs.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
questions.explanationMarkdown:
