● India has taken a major step towards sustainable and self-reliant infrastructure with the successful transfer of an **indigenous bio-bitumen technology** developed by the CSIR–**Central Road Research Institute** **(CSIR-CRRI),** New Delhi and the CSIR–**Indian Institute of Petroleum (CSIR-IIP),** Dehradun.
● The bio-bitumen technology involves the **collection of rice straw (organic matter, post harvest),** its pelletisation and subsequent conversion into **bio-oil through pyrolysis,** which is then blended with conventional bitumen. Studies have suggested that **20–30% of petroleum-based bitumen** can be replaced without affecting road performance. **So, statement I is correct and statement II is not correct.**
● **India** has become the **first country** to scale bio-bitumen technology to both industrial and commercial levels within the same year. Biomass pyrolysis under this technology produces multiple value streams, including **bio-binders for road construction, gaseous fuels, bio-pesticide fractions** and **high-grade** **carbon for advanced applications.** This multiplicity of outputs enhances the cost-effectiveness and longterm viability of the process. The approach also underscores the need for policy-level blending mandates to facilitate nationwide adoption of bio-bitumen technology. **So, statement III is correct.** **Therefore, option (c) is the correct answer.**
**Relevance:** Recently, the Minister of Road Transport and Highways has inaugurated the country’s first sustainable road made at the Nagpur-Mansar Bypass Project, NH44, using lignin-based bio-bitumen.

questions.subject: Science & Technology
questions.microTopic: IT, Computers, Space, NanoTech, BioTech, Robotics, Defence, Nuclear, General Science
questions.questionText:
Which one of the following best describes “the
Mpemba effect”?

options.a:
A phenomenon in which hot water freezes
faster than cold water under certain conditions
options.b:
A phenomenon in which the boiling point of a
liquid increases with increasing pressure
options.c:
A phenomenon describing the anomalous
expansion of water, resulting in maximum density at 4°C
options.d:
A phenomenon in which supercooled water
remains liquid below its freezing point

questions.explanationMarkdown:
● The **Mpemba effect is a counterintuitive thermodynamic phenomenon in which hot water, under** **certain controlled conditions, freezes faster than cold water.** Although first observed centuries ago, it has gained renewed scientific attention in recent years due to controlled laboratory experiments aimed at understanding its underlying mechanisms. Recent studies suggest that factors such as enhanced evaporation, stronger convection currents, reduced dissolved gases, and differences in molecular hydrogen-bond structures may accelerate heat loss in initially warmer water, allowing it to freeze more rapidly under specific circumstances.
● Modern experimental research has moved beyond simple observation and has explored the microscopic behaviour of water molecules, particularly how prior heating alters hydrogen-bond networks and influences cooling dynamics. Understanding this effect has **potential applications in cryopreservation**, **climate science (ice formation processes)**, **industrial cooling systems**, and **improving the efficiency of** **freezing technologies, among others**.
● Researchers at the **Jawaharlal Nehru Centre for Advanced Scientific Research (JNCASR)**, have used **supercomputers** to develop the **first detailed simulation of ice formation**, providing strong evidence for the **Mpemba effect**. They used **supercomputer-based simulations** to study the **Mpemba effect.** **Therefore, option (a) is the correct answer.**
**Relevance:** Researchers at the Jawaharlal Nehru Centre for Advanced Scientific Research used supercomputers to simulate ice formation, confirming the Mpemba effect.

questions.subject: Environment
questions.microTopic: Ramsar Sites, World Heritage Sites, UNESCO Heritage Sites, WHS, Biodiversity Heritage Sites, etc.
questions.questionText:
Consider the following statements:
I. 	 It is located in the Indian Ocean.
II. 	 It is recognised as a UNESCO World Heritage
Site.
III. 	Most of its reptile species and land snail
species do not occur anywhere else in the world.
Which is this island?
questions.explanationMarkdown:
● **Socotra Island** is located in the **Indian Ocean** that belongs to Yemen.  It is the **largest island in a small** **archipelago** near the  **Horn of Africa**, covering an area of nearly 3,600 sq km.
● The island’s interior is dominated by the **Hajhir Mountains.**h. It separated from the Arabian Peninsula around 80 million years ago, a long isolation that has earned it the title **“Galápagos of the Indian Ocean.”**
● Socotra is of exceptional global ecological importance due to its high level of endemism: about **37% of its** **plant species**, **90% of reptile species** **and 95% of land snail species are** **found** nowhere else in the world. The island also supports significant populations of land and marine birds. In recognition of its outstanding biodiversity, **Socotra** **was** **designated a UNESCO World** **Heritage Site in 2008.** **Therefore, option (a) is the correct answer.**
**Relevance:** Hundreds of tourists remain stranded on the Yemeni island of Socotra after flights were suspended due to clashes on the mainland.

questions.subject: Polity
questions.questionText:
The ‘PAIMANA’ portal, recently seen in the news,
is a platform that:

options.b:
Monitors only completed Central Sector
infrastructure projects
options.c:
Serves as a centralised repository for tracking
Central Sector infrastructure projects
options.d:
Replaces an existing project monitoring
portal

questions.explanationMarkdown:
● The **Ministry of Statistics and Programme Implementation (MoSPI)** has launched **PAIMANA** **(Project Assessment, Infrastructure Monitoring & Analytics for Nation-building),** a web-based portal for the **mandatory monitoring of Central Sector infrastructure projects costing ₹150 crore** **and above.** It replaces the earlier (Online Computerised Monitoring system)-2006 system.
● Adhering to the principle of **“one data, one entry”**, PAIMANA is **integrated with the Department for** **Promotion of Industry and Internal Trade’s (DPIIT’s) Integrated Project Monitoring Portal** (IPMP/IIG-PMG) through APIs, enabling automatic data transfer from Central Ministries and Departments. As a result, numerous  projects are updated automatically, significantly reducing manual data entry and improving standardisation and efficiency.
● PAIMANA serves as a **centralised national repository of infrastructure projects** and supports webbased analytical reporting to enhance data accuracy and operational effectiveness. Its key features include advanced analytics, role-based access, interactive dashboards, reporting and query modules and review
mechanisms to identify data gaps and improve data quality. The portal has been operationalised through close coordination with over **20 Central Ministries/Departments, the Department of Economic Affairs** **(DEA) and DPIIT,** covering project mapping, data migration, analytics, and validation. **Therefore, option (c) is the correct answer.**
**Relevance:** Recently, the Ministry of Statistics and Programme Implementation (MoSPI) has operationalised a PAIMANA portal for the monitoring of Central Sector Infrastructure Projects.

questions.subject: History
questions.questionText:
Which one of the following sites is **not** a part of
the “Diamond Triangle” of Buddhist heritage sites
in Odisha?
questions.explanationMarkdown:
● The **Diamond Triangle of Odisha** comprises three prominent Buddhist hill complexes, **Ratnagiri,** **Lalitgiri and Udaygiri,** located in the **Jajpur–Cuttack region of southeastern Odisha.**
● **Ratnagiri,** meaning **“Hill of Jewels”**, is the most extensively excavated Buddhist site in the state. Excavations of its **5th–13th century Buddhist complex** have revealed significant remains, including a **colossal Buddha head**, a **massive palm, ancient structural walls and inscribed relics** dating to the 8th– 9th centuries CE.
● **Lalitgiri**, locally known as **Naltigiri,** is regarded as the **oldest site** of the Diamond Triangle. A major archaeological discovery here is a relic casket consisting of four nested containers made of **khondalite,** **steatite, silver and gold**, believed to have housed relics of **Lord Buddha.**
● **Udaygiri,** the **largest** of the three sites, is located between the foothills of two ranges of the **Eastern** **Ghats.** It is distinct from the Udaygiri–Khandagiri caves near Bhubaneswar. Unlike Ratnagiri and Lalitgiri, **no sculptures or inscriptions associated with the Vajrayana** tantric tradition have been found at this site so far.
● **Dhauli** is situated on the banks of the **Daya River** in Odisha and is an important **historical and Buddhist** **site**. It is traditionally associated with **Emperor Ashoka’s renunciation of war after the Kalinga War** **(265 BCE)**. The site is noted for its **Ashokan rock edicts**, an early **rock-cut elephant sculpture** and the **white-domed Shanti Stupa (Peace Pagoda)**. It is not a part of the “Diamond Triangle” of Buddhist heritage sites in Odisha. **Therefore, option (d) is the answer.**
**Knowledge Box**
● **Ratnagiri** is regarded as a major early centre of **Vajrayana (Tantrayana) Buddhism**, a school that emphasised the attainment of mystical powers through the Vajra, symbolising a thunderbolt or diamond. This association gives rise to the term “Diamond Triangle” for the three prominent Buddhist sites of Odisha.
● The Ratnagiri monastery is unique in India for its **curvilinear roof architecture.**
**Relevance:** Recently, UNESCO has included the ‘Diamond Triangle' of Ratnagiri, Udayagiri and Lalitgiri Buddhist sites in its tentative India list for consideration of ‘World Heritage Site’ status.

questions.subject: International Relations
questions.microTopic: International Organizations (UN, IMF, World Bank, ASEAN, BRICS, etc.)
questions.questionText:
With reference to BRICS group, consider the
following statements:
I. 	 It is an international organisation with a
permanent secretariat and legally binding
decision making authority.
II. 	 India assumed the presidency of BRICS in
2026.
III. 	Egypt, Ethiopia, and Indonesia have been
admitted as full members of BRICS.
How many of the above statements are correct?
questions.explanationMarkdown:
● The **BRICS group is an intergovernmental forum** of emerging economies that aims to enhance cooperation on political, economic, and development issues. **It was originally formed by Brazil, Russia,** **India, China, and South Africa,** and operates as an **informal coordination mechanism** with a rotating
presidency and consensus‑based decision‑making. **The grouping does not have a permanent** **secretariat, treaty‑based governance, or legally binding authority over its members. So, statement I** **is not correct.**
○ The BRICS is **not an international organisation with a permanent secretariat or legally** **binding decision‑making authority**. It functions as a forum for diplomatic consultation and cooperation, with decisions taken by consensus, but lacks a fixed institutional structure like those found in treaty‑based organisations.
● **India** assumed the **BRICS presidency in 2026**, taking over the rotating chairmanship from Brazil on 1 January 2026. The presidency rotates annually among members and is responsible for steering the grouping’s priorities and hosting the summit during its tenure. As part of this transition, the External Affairs Minister of India unveiled the **official logo and the theme** for the year — **“Building for** **Resilience, Innovation, Cooperation and Sustainability”** — and launched the BRICS India 2026 website, thereby setting the agenda for India’s leadership year.  **So, statement II is correct.**
● **Egypt, Ethiopia, and Indonesia have been admitted as full members of BRICS** as part of the group’s expansion. Egypt and Ethiopia joined as full members in 2024, and Indonesia officially became a member in early 2025. **So, statement III is correct.** **So, only two of the above statements are correct.** **Therefore, option (b) is the correct answer.**
**Relevance:** India assumed the BRICS presidency on January 1, 2026.

questions.subject: Science & Technology
questions.questionText:
With regard to sex determination in insects,
consider the following:
**Statement I:**
In some insects, males develop from unfertilized
eggs while females develop from fertilized eggs.
**Statement II:**
Males inherit only one set of chromosomes from
their mother, while females inherit two sets, one
from each parent.
Which one of the following is correct in respect of
the above statements?

options.a:
Both Statement I and Statement II are correct
and Statement II explains Statement I
options.b:
Both Statement I and Statement II are correct
but Statement II does not explain Statement I
options.c:
Statement I is correct but Statement II is not
correct
options.d:
Statement I is not correct but Statement II is
correct

questions.explanationMarkdown:
● In some insects, males develop from **unfertilized eggs**, while females develop from **fertilized eggs**. This means that males inherit only the genetic material from their mother, whereas females inherit **one set of** **chromosomes from the mother and one from the father**. This system is common in insects like **ants,** **bees, and wasps**. **So, statement I is correct.**
● Because male insects carry only **one set of chromosomes**, any change in their genes is immediately visible in their body. In contrast, females have two sets, so one set can mask minor changes in the other. This explains why males are genetically “haploid” and females “diploid,” which directly affects how traits are passed on and expressed. **So, statement II is correct.**
○ Mitochondria, the tiny energy-producing structures in cells, are **inherited only from the mother**. Male insects do not pass their mitochondria to their offspring, which is why mitochondrial DNA evolution is influenced mainly by females. **So, both Statement I and Statement II are correct and Statement II explains Statement I** **Therefore, option (a) is the correct answer.**
**Relevance:** The results of a new study show how the way a species produces its males and females can shape the path of mitochondrial evolution, revealing an unexpected connection between reproductive biology and diversity.

questions.subject: Science & Technology
questions.questionText:
With reference to the BioE3 (Biotechnology for
Economy, Environment and Employment) Policy,
consider the following thematic areas:
I. 	 Bio-based Chemicals and Enzymes
II. 	 Functional Foods and Smart Proteins
III. 	Precision Biotherapeutics
IV. 	 Climate-Resilient Agriculture
V. 	 Carbon Capture and Its Utilization
VI. 	Futuristic Marine and Space Research
Which of the above are included as thematic areas
under the BioE3 policy?

options.a: I, II, III and IV only
options.b: I, II, IV and V only
options.c: I, III, IV, V and VI only

questions.explanationMarkdown:
● The **BioE3 (Biotechnology for Economy, Environment, and Employment) Policy** is an initiative by the Government of India aimed at leveraging biotechnology to drive economic growth, create
employment, and address environmental challenges. It seeks to promote innovation and research across multiple sectors where biotechnology can have a transformative impact.
● One of the key thematic areas is **Bio-based Chemicals and Enzymes**, which focuses on developing sustainable alternatives to conventional chemicals and industrial processes using biotechnological methods. Another important area is **Functional Foods and Smart Proteins**, which targets the development of nutritionally enhanced foods and alternative protein sources to improve health outcomes and support food security. **So, points I and II are correct.**
● **Precision Biotherapeutics** is also included, emphasizing advanced medical biotechnology for targeted treatments and personalized healthcare solutions. **Climate-Resilient Agriculture** forms a critical component, aiming to improve crop resistance to environmental stresses through biotechnological interventions, ensuring food security in the face of climate change. **So, points III and IV are correct.**
● Additionally, **Carbon Capture and Its Utilization** is recognized as a thematic area under the policy, highlighting technologies that reduce greenhouse gas emissions and convert captured carbon into useful products. These focus areas collectively reflect the multi-sectoral vision of the BioE3 policy to integrate biotechnology with economy, environment, and employment objectives. **So, points V and VI are correct.** **Therefore, option (d) is the correct answer.**
**Knowledge Box:**
● BioE3 Policy (2024): Biotechnology for Economy, Environment and Employment
● Core approach: Convergence of biotech, digital tools and engineering
● CRA tools: Biofertilizers, biopesticides, soil microbiome analysis, genome editing
● Six BioE3 thematic sectors: Bio-based chemicals & enzymes; functional foods & smart proteins; precision biotherapeutics; climate-resilient agriculture; carbon capture & utilisation; futuristic marine & space research
● Policy vision: Net-zero alignment, Atmanirbhar Bharat, Viksit Bharat @2047
**Relevance:** Union Cabinet approved BioE3 (Biotechnology for Economy, Environment and Employment) policy, a framework that creates a convergence between biotech, engineering and digitalization for building a more equitable and sustainable future.

questions.subject: Economy
questions.questionText:
With reference to the U.S. dollar and monetary
policy dynamics, consider the following statements:
I. 	 The U.S. dollar typically weakens when the
Federal Reserve cuts interest rates.
II. 	 As global growth rates converge and other
major economies adopt tighter monetary policies, the U.S. dollar may face downward pressure.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
questions.explanationMarkdown:
**Answer: c**
● **Explanation:** When the **Federal Reserve (the U.S. central bank) cuts interest rates**, it lowers the returns investors earn on dollar‑denominated financial assets. This tends to make the dollar less attractive compared to other currencies, leading investors to move money into assets that offer relatively higher returns. As a result, the demand for the dollar falls and its value can weaken. This relationship explains why the dollar typically weakens when the Federal Reserve cuts interest rates. **So, statement I is correct.**
● Apart from domestic monetary policy, the **global economic environment** also affects the dollar’s strength. If economies around the world grow at similar rates and other major central banks adopt **tighter** **monetary policies** (for example, keeping their interest rates higher or slowing their rate cuts), then financial returns outside the United States can become more attractive relative to U.S. assets. In such a situation, international investors may reduce their holdings of dollar assets and increase investments in
other currencies or markets. This shift can also put **downward pressure on the U.S. dollar**. **So, statement** **II is correct.**
● Taken together, both domestic interest rate moves by the Fed and the relative monetary policy stance of other major economies help determine the direction of the U.S. dollar. In the present context, lower U.S. interest rates and tighter policy abroad can weaken the dollar. **Therefore, option (c) is the correct answer.**
**Relevance:** The U.S. dollar slumped more than 9% this year, against a basket of currencies, opening a new tab, its worst showing in eight years, driven by expectations of Federal Reserve rate cuts.

questions.subject: Science & Technology
questions.microTopic: Information and Communication Technology, Robotics, AI
questions.questionText:
Extreme ultraviolet (EUV) lithography is the
process used for:

options.a:
Increasing the electrical conductivity of
semiconductor materials
options.b:
Etching ultra-fine circuit patterns on silicon
wafers for chip manufacturing
options.c:
Packaging and thermal management of
finished semiconductor chips
options.d:
Testing and certification of integrated circuits
after fabrication

questions.explanationMarkdown:
● **Extreme Ultraviolet (EUV) lithography** is a cutting-edge technology used in semiconductor manufacturing. It involves the use of **extreme ultraviolet light with a very short wavelength (around**
**13.5 nanometers)** to etch ultra-fine patterns onto silicon wafers. These patterns form the intricate circuits and transistors that make up modern microchips.
● The **short wavelength of EUV light** allows chipmakers to produce much smaller and more densely packed features compared to older photolithography techniques that use longer wavelengths. This enables the production of advanced chips with higher performance, lower power consumption, and increased computational capabilities.
● EUV lithography is **strategically important** for the semiconductor industry because it underpins the manufacturing of next-generation processors, memory chips, and other high-performance integrated circuits. It is a key enabler for technological self-reliance and maintaining global competitiveness in the semiconductor supply chain. **Therefore, option (b) is the correct answer.**
**Relevance:** EUV lithography is strategically important for global semiconductor supply chains, export controls, and technological self-reliance.

questions.subject: Geography
questions.questionText:
Consider the following:
I. 	 Indications of Primitive Traits
II. 	 Distinctive Culture
III. 	Geographical Isolation
IV. 	 Backwardness
V. 	 Population size of the group
VI. 	Literacy rate of the group
How many of the above are the criteria proposed by the Lokur Committee to include a tribal group into Schedule Tribe category?
questions.explanationMarkdown:
● In 1965, the Ministry of Law appointed the **Advisory Committee on the Revision of Lists of Scheduled** **Castes and Scheduled Tribes**, chaired by **B.N. Lokur**. Tasked with refining the lists following the 1961 Census, the committee sought to create an objective framework to distinguish genuine tribal communities from other social groups. This ensured that affirmative action reached those truly isolated from the developmental mainstream.
● The committee established **five distinct criteria** that remain the "gold standard" for the Government of India today. These include **indications of primitive traits**, **distinctive culture**, **geographical isolation**, **shyness of contact with the community at large**, and **general backwardness**. These benchmarks focus on historical and social characteristics rather than modern statistical metrics like current population size or literacy rates. **So, points I, II, III, and IV are correct and points V and VI are not correct.**
● Today, these criteria guide a rigorous multi-layered approval process involving the **Registrar General of** **India (RGI)** and the **National Commission for Scheduled Tribes (NCST)**. Under **Article 342** of the Constitution, these five pillars must be satisfied before the Union Cabinet can propose a bill in Parliament to amend the **Constitution (Scheduled Tribes) Order, 1950**, and officially include a new community.
**Therefore, option (b) is the correct answer.**
**Relevance:** The Coordination Committee of Tribal Organisations of Assam (CCTOA) has rejected the "illegal and unconstitutional" recommendations of the Group of Ministers to grant Scheduled Tribe (ST) status to six communities.

questions.subject: Environment
questions.microTopic: Hazardous, Radioactive, E-Waste, Heavy Metals, etc.
questions.questionText:
Consider the following:
I. 	 Wood preservatives
II. 	 Cultivation of rice
III. 	Smelting of gold
IV. 	 Volcanic activity
How many of the above cause Arsenic pollution?
questions.explanationMarkdown:
● **Wood is treated** with chromated copper arsenate, an arsenic and chromium containing preservative, **to** **prevent deterioration** from fungal attack. Eventually the metals in the preservative may leach out of the wood. This accounts for about 70% of global arsenic use and a large source of arsenic to the environment. **So, point I is correct.**
● **Rice** is particularly efficient at absorbing arsenic from soil and water compared to other cereal crops. Because rice is a staple food for a large portion of the global population, it is recognized by health organizations (like the WHO and FAO) as a major dietary source of inorganic arsenic exposure.
o In rice fields **pollution occurs primarily when paddy fields are irrigated with arsenic-rich** **groundwater**, leading to arsenic buildup in the soil and its subsequent uptake by the rice plants.. **So, point II is correct.**
● **Smelting and mining operations for metals** like gold, copper, and lead are major anthropogenic sources of arsenic. These **processes often involve heating arsenic-bearing ores**, which releases arsenic oxide dust and other toxic byproducts into the air and water. **So, point III is correct.**
● **Volcanic activity** is a significant **natural (geogenic) source of arsenic.** Volcanic eruptions and geothermal activity release arsenic into the atmosphere and contribute to high arsenic concentrations in surrounding rocks and groundwater. **So, point IV is correct.** **Therefore**, **option (d) is the correct answer.**
**Knowledge Box**
**About Arsenic:**
● **Arsenic** is a naturally occurring, semi metallic element widely distributed in the Earth’s crust. It exists in various chemical forms and each of its chemical forms possesses different mobility, which accounts for its toxicity. As**3+** (arsenite) and As**5+** (arsenate) are most stable as well as toxic forms of arsenic.
**Straighthead disease of rice:**
● Straighthead disease is a physiological disorder in rice with symptoms of sterile spikelets, distorted husks, and erect panicles.
● Its **primary cause is arsenic toxicity,** specifically methylated arsenic species such as dimethylarsinic acid (DMA). It is common in soils with high arsenic residues. The condition can result in up to 70% yield losses in severely affected areas.
**Arsenicosis:**
● Chronic arsenic **exposure from contaminated water and food** causes arsenicosis, a condition characterized by skin lesions (hyperkeratosis, pigmentation changes) and a significantly high risk of developing cancers of the skin, lungs, bladder, and kidneys. It also causes cardiovascular diseases, diabetes, neurological issues, and adverse pregnancy outcomes.
**Relevance:** New research reveals that rice paddy microbes dictate arsenic toxicity and crop losses.

questions.subject: Polity
questions.sectionGroup: Part V & VI: The Union and The States
questions.questionText:
With reference to the Governor’s Address to
the State Legislature, consider the following statements:
I. 	 The Governor is constitutionally mandated
to address the State Legislature at the commencement of the first session of each year.
II. 	 The Governor is constitutionally bound to
read each part of the address as approved by the Council of Ministers without any omissions or additions.
III. 	If a motion of thanks on the Governor's
address fails to pass in the legislature, then the ruling government must resign.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
questions.explanationMarkdown:
● The **Governor’s Address** to the State Legislature is a key constitutional practice under **Article 176** of the Constitution of India. **At the start of the first session after each general election, and every year** **thereafter, the Governor addresses the Legislature**—either the Assembly alone or both Houses in bicameral States. Like the **President’s Address under Article 87** of the Constitution of India, the **Governor’s** speech is **drafted by the Council of Ministers and outlines the government’s policies and** **programmes.** **So, statement I is correct.**
● While convention dictates the Governor should read the address exactly as prepared by the Council of Ministers, the statement is not strictly correct in law. **Under Article 176,** the Governor is **mandated to** **deliver the address**, but the **Constitution of India does not explicitly "bind" them to every word.** Judicial precedents (such as the **Syed Abdul Mansur Habibullah case**) suggest that **if a Governor omits** **or skips portions of the speech, it is treated as a procedural irregularity rather than a constitutional** **illegality** that would void the session. Consequently, while the Governor acts on the "aid and advice" of the Cabinet, a refusal to read specific parts does not legally invalidate the proceedings. **So, statement II** **is not correct.**
● Following the Governor’s address, the Legislature discusses the **Motion of Thank**s, which functions as a vote of confidence in the government. **If this motion fails in the Legislative Assembly, it indicates that** **the ruling government has lost the support of the majority.** By constitutional convention, **this** **situation obliges the Council of Ministers to resign**. **So, statement III is correct.** **Therefore**, **option (a) is the correct answer.**
**Relevance:** Tamil Nadu Governor declined to read out his customary speech prepared by the State government on the floor of the State Legislative Assembly on January 20, 2026, and walked out of the House.

questions.subject: Agriculture
questions.questionText:
With reference to the ‘Root Wilt Disease’, recently
seen in news, consider the following statements:
I. 	 The disease is primarily transmitted through
insect vectors, such as the lacewing bug and the plant hopper.
II. 	 Erratic temperature extremes, and the rise
of biotic stress from emerging pests, have significantly accelerated its spread.
III. 	Once a tree is infested, symptoms appear
immediately and the tree quickly becomes unproductive and sheds all its nuts.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
questions.explanationMarkdown:
● **Root wilt disease**, caused by **phytoplasma**, is one of the most devastating diseases affecting coconut palms. It primarily leads to reduced productivity rather than immediate death and has become a significant concern for coconut cultivation in India, especially in Kerala and other southern states. The disease affects various parts of the palm, particularly the leaves and roots, causing wilting, drooping, and flaccidity, along with ribbing, paling, and necrosis of leaflets.
● The root wilt disease is transmitted through insect vectors. The major vectors include the **plant hopper** **(Proutista moesta)** and the **lacewing bug (Stephanitis typica)**, which carry the phytoplasma in their salivary glands and phloem, facilitating its spread among coconut palms.  **So, statement I is correct.**
● Environmental and biotic factors have contributed to the increased incidence of the disease. **Erratic** **temperature extremes** and the rise of **biotic stress from emerging pests** have accelerated the spread and severity of root wilt disease by weakening palms and increasing vulnerability to vector-mediated transmission. As recently witnessed in Kerala. **So, statement II is correct.**
● Root wilt is a **non-lethal, debilitating disease**. Unlike some foliar diseases, symptoms do not appear immediately, and affected trees do not shed all their nuts or become instantly unproductive. The disease gradually reduces productivity over time. **So, statement III is not correct.** **Therefore**, **option (c) is the correct answer.**
**Relevance:** Researchers have standardized integrated cultivation practice and are focusing on developing resistant and tolerant varieties of coconut to mitigate root wilt disease.

questions.subject: Environment
questions.microTopic: Major Species (Keystone, Indicator, Invasive, Endemic, etc.)
questions.questionText:
This species, found in the forests of Madagascar,
weaves silk that outperforms steel and most human-made fibres in both strength and toughness. Its silk has a tensile strength that is higher than that of iron, making it the toughest biological material ever tested.
Which is this species?
questions.explanationMarkdown:
● **Darwin's bark spider (Caerostris darwini)**, is found in the forests of **Madagascar**. It is renowned for producing silk **that surpasses steel and most human-made fibres** in both **strength and toughness**, with a tensile strength of about **1.6 gigapascals**, roughly three times that of iron. This makes it the **toughest** **biological material ever tested**. The silk forms the structural backbone of its **orb webs**, which are often the largest ever recorded, spanning rivers and streams to capture flying insects efficiently.
● This extraordinary silk is primarily produced by **large adult female spiders**. **Females of this species are** **significantly larger than males and juveniles, and only they synthesize silk of exceptional toughness,** **stiffness, and energy absorption.** This high-performance silk is metabolically costly to produce and is ‘switched on’ as **females grow and take on the ecological role of constructing huge webs capable of** **intercepting fast-moving prey.** Males and juveniles, in contrast, produce silk that is mechanically weaker, though equally stretchable, demonstrating that toughness is tuned according to body size and functional need.
● The **web architecture** of Darwin’s bark spiders complements the silk’s mechanical properties. Adult females build sparser webs with wider gaps, using less silk per unit area, yet the webs can withstand enormous forces. This adaptation balances energy expenditure with structural efficiency, allowing the spider to capture prey that few other spiders can. The combination of **body size, sex, ecology, and** **behaviour** thus explains why Darwin’s bark spider produces silk that is unparalleled in the natural world. **Therefore**, **option (a) is the correct answer.**
**Relevance:** Darwin's bark spider, found in the forests of Madagascar, weaves silk that outperforms steel and most human-made fibres.

questions.subject: Geography
questions.microTopic: Critical and Strategic Minerals (Lithium, Cobalt, Titanium, KABIL, REEs, etc.)
questions.questionText:
With reference to copper, consider the following
statements:
I. 	 India meets most of its domestic demand for
copper through imports.
II. 	 Chile has the largest copper reserves in the
world.
III. 	China is the largest producer of refined copper
globally.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
questions.explanationMarkdown:
● **Copper** is a critical industrial metal with wide applications in electrical equipment, construction, and renewable energy technologies. While India has significant copper resources, it shifted from being a net exporter to a **net importer** of refined copper around 2018 (following the closure of the Sterlite Copper plant in Tuticorin). In India, domestic production of copper is insufficient to meet demand, and as a result, **most of the country’s copper requirements are met through imports**, making the nation heavily dependent on global supply chains. **So, statement I is correct.**
● Chile holds the **largest copper reserves in the world**, with major deposits such as **Escondida** and **Collahuasi**. These abundant reserves have enabled Chile to maintain a dominant position in global copper production, accounting for roughly a **quarter of total mine output worldwide**. Its resource base and mining infrastructure make it a key player in both the supply of copper and long-term global market stability. **So, statement II is correct.**
● While China is not the largest producer of copper ore, it is the **largest producer of refined copper**, processing both domestic and imported copper to meet the high demand from its industrial, construction, and electronics sectors. This gives China a commanding position in the global copper value chain, though it does not surpass Chile in **mine output**. **So, statement III is correct.** **Therefore**, **option (d) is the correct answer.**
**Relevance:** The global EV boom is accelerating a copper crunch.

questions.subject: Economy
questions.questionText:
With reference to the Urban Cooperative
Banks (UCBs) in India, consider the following statements:
I. 	 They are under the direct supervision and
regulatory control of the Reserve Bank of India (RBI).
II. 	 The RBI has incorporated the National Urban
Co-operative Finance and Development Corporation Limited to provide Information Technology (IT) infrastructure to ULBs.
III. 	Urban Cooperative Banks are permitted
to issue credit cards and provide internet banking facilities.
IV. 	 They are excluded from the coverage of
depositor’s insurance schemes under Deposit Insurance and Credit Guarantee Corporation.
Which of the statements given above are correct?
questions.explanationMarkdown:
**Answer: d** **Explanation**:
● The term Urban Co-operative Banks (UCBs), though not formally defined, refers to primary cooperative banks located in urban and semi-urban areas. **Following the Banking Regulation (Amendment) Act,** **2020**, UCBs were **brought under the direct supervision and full regulatory control of the Reserve** **Bank of India (RBI)** regarding banking functions, management, and audit. While the Registrar of Cooperative Societies still handles administrative registration, the RBI now holds the primary "reins" over their financial health.
o RBI may prescribe conditions on and qualifications for employment of Chairman of co-operative banks.  RBI may remove a chairman not meeting ‘fit and proper’ criteria and appoint a suitable person. o RBI may supersede the Board of Directors of a co-operative bank after consultation with the state government. **So, statement I is correct.**
● **National Urban Co-operative Finance and Development Corporation Limited (NUCFDC)** was established to act as an Umbrella Organization for the UCB sector and provide IT infrastructure. It is incorporated **as a specialized Non-Banking Finance Company (NBFC)** by the UCBs themselves and the National Federation of Urban Co-operative Banks and Credit Societies (NAFCUB).
o NUCFDC has introduced **Sahakar DigiPay for UPI**, aiming to enable all UCBs to adopt modern, secure digital payment methods. **So, statement II is not correct.**
● Financially sound UCBs with a minimum net worth of ₹100 crore and Core Banking Solution (CBS) can issue credit cards to members, while UCBs with at least ₹50 crore net worth, 10% CRAR, and low NPAs can offer internet banking with prior RBI approval. These facilities must follow strict RBI, 2025 guidelines. **So, statement III is correct.**
● **Deposit Insurance and Credit Guarantee Corporation (DICGC)** insures various types of deposits up to ₹ 5,00,000 per depositor per bank (including principal and interest) for all cooperative banks. DICGC is a subsidiary of the Reserve Bank of India. **So, statement IV is not correct.** **Therefore**, **option (d) is the correct answer.**
**Knowledge Box**
**Few Facts**
● As per RBI and NABARD, there are 1,457 Urban Cooperative Banks, 34 State Co- operative Banks (StCBs), 351 District Central Co-operative Banks (DCCBs) and one Industrial Co- operative Bank viz. (Tamil Nadu Industrial Co-operative Bank) which is under the supervision of RBI and NABARD.
● Maharashtra has the highest number of cooperative banks followed by Karnataka and Gujarat.
**Relevance:** An internal working group of the central bank has suggested new eligibility parameters for granting urban cooperative banking (UCB) licences.

questions.subject: Science & Technology
questions.questionText:
Consider the following statements:
I. 	 A ramjet engine is a more efficient propulsion
system for flight within the atmosphere than a rocket engine.
II. 	 Scramjet engines are capable of efficient
operation only when the airflow inside the combustor remains supersonic.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
questions.explanationMarkdown:
**Answer: c** **Explanation**:
● A ramjet engine is an air-breathing jet engine that requires no moving parts. Instead, it relies on the aircraft’s forward motion to draw external air into a specially shaped intake passage for compression and combustion.
o **A ramjet engine is more efficient than a rocket for atmospheric flight because it is an "air-** **breathing" engine.** A rocket must carry both its fuel and its oxidizer (like liquid oxygen), which adds massive weight. A ramjet takes its oxygen directly from the surrounding atmosphere, allowing it to carry more fuel or payload for the same weight, resulting in a much higher specific impulse within the atmosphere. o Ramjets are the most efficient at speeds higher than Mach 2. Ramjets cannot produce static thrust; in simple terms, without the aircraft already moving at high speeds, ramjets cannot generate thrust since ramjets cannot compress air. **So, statement I is correct.**
● A **scramjet engine** is an improvement over the ramjet engine as it efficiently operates at **hypersonic** **speeds (At or above MACH 5 speed)** and allows supersonic combustion. Thus, it is known as **Supersonic** **Combustion Ramjet, or Scramjet**. By maintaining supersonic airflow throughout the combustion process, it offers higher efficiency than conventional rockets, making it ideal for hypersonic cruise missiles and reusable space launch vehicles. **So, statement II is correct.** **Therefore**, **option (c) is the correct answer.**
**Relevance:** The Hyderabad-based Defence Research & Development Laboratory (DRDL) of DRDO successfully conducted a long-duration ground test of its Actively Cooled Scramjet Full-Scale Combustor.

questions.subject: International Relations
questions.microTopic: Global Groupings and Agreements (FTA, Paris Agreement, NPT, QUAD, etc.)
questions.questionText:
Which of the following countries are members of
the Weimar Triangle?
I. 	 Greece
II. 	 Italy
III. 	Poland
IV. 	 Austria
V. 	 Germany
VI. 	France
Select the correct answer using the code given below:
questions.explanationMarkdown:
**Answer: a** **Explanation**:
● The **Weimar Triangle** is a trilateral political cooperation format established in **1991 in Weimar,** **Germany**, to strengthen consultation and coordination on European and foreign policy issues in the post‑Cold War period. It brings together **France, Germany, and Poland**, with the aim of promoting dialogue on European integration, regional security, and diplomatic collaboration. Over the years, this framework has been used to address major European challenges and coordinate positions on EU and NATO matters. Other countries such as Greece, Italy, and Austria are **not part of this grouping**, although they are significant European states. **So, points III, V and VI are correct and points I, II, IV are not** **correct.** **Therefore**, **option (a) is the correct answer.**
**Relevance:** External affairs Minister of India participated in India’s first-ever engagement in the Weimar Triangle, with French and Polish counterparts along with German representatives.

questions.subject: Economy
questions.questionText:
The Directorate General of Mines Safety (DGMS)
functions under:
questions.explanationMarkdown:
**Answer: a** **Explanation**:
● **The Directorate General of Mines Safety (DGMS), established in 1902 under the provisions of the** **Indian Mines Act, 1901**, is the apex regulatory body responsible for ensuring the **safety, health, and** **welfare of mine workers** across India. Under the **Mines Act, 1952**, DGMS prescribes uniform standards for mine operations, conducts inspections, investigates accidents, and enforces compliance with statutory requirements. It plays a vital role in promoting the well-being of mine workers and contributes to the **sustainable development of the mining sector**, making it a key institution in India’s industrial and labor safety framework.
● DGMS functions under the **administrative control of the Ministry of Labour and Employment**, with its **headquarters in Dhanbad, Jharkhand**. The organization is headed by the **Director General of** **Mines Safety** and operates through **eight zonal offices** across the country, each managed by a Deputy
Director General of Mines Safety. This structure allows DGMS to effectively monitor and regulate mine safety standards nationwide.
● In addition to regulatory functions, DGMS is entrusted with **promoting awareness of labor codes and** **modern safety practices** in mining. It plays a central role in implementing the **Occupational Safety,** **Health and Working Conditions (OSH) Code** within the sector, leveraging technology and training programs to enhance safety compliance. By coordinating with the Ministry of Labour and Employment, DGMS ensures that mines operate under safer, healthier, and more sustainable conditions, thereby safeguarding workers while supporting the growth of India’s mining industry. **Therefore**, **option (a) is the correct answer.**
**Relevance:** DGMS Marks 125 Years of Commitment to Mines Safety and Workers’ Welfare.

questions.subject: Science & Technology
questions.questionText:
With reference to "Novel Biomaterials," consider
the following statements:
I. 	 They possess self-healing capabilities for
autonomous repair of structural micro-cracks or damage.
II. 	 They can actively interact with and integrate
into living biological tissues.
III. 	They are capable of altering their physical or
chemical properties in response to external environmental changes.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
questions.explanationMarkdown:
**Answer: a** **Explanation**:
● **Novel biomaterials** are advanced materials derived from biological sources or engineered using biological processes that offer properties not found in conventional materials. Unlike “drop-in” or “dropout” biomaterials, **novel biomaterials are designed to perform specialised functions** such as **interacting with living tissues, adapting to environmental changes, or repairing themselves.** They are increasingly used in healthcare, regenerative medicine, and advanced material engineering.
● Novel biomaterials are engineered with **self-healing capabilities**, **enabling them to autonomously** **repair structural micro-cracks or minor damage.** This is particularly useful in medical implants, coatings, and advanced composites where **durability and longevity** are essential. **Self-healing** **mechanisms may be triggered by biological or chemical processes** that restore structural integrity without external intervention. **So statement I is correct.**
● Novel biomaterials can be **bioactive**, meaning **they actively interact with and integrate into living** **tissues.** In medical applications such as implants, scaffolds, sutures, and prosthetics, these materials support cell attachment, growth, and tissue regeneration. Their compatibility with biological systems makes them crucial in fields like tissue engineering and regenerative medicine. **So, statement II is** **correct.**
● Certain novel biomaterials are **stimuli-responsive**, meaning **they can alter their physical or chemical** **properties in response to external environmental changes such as temperature, pH, moisture, or** **mechanical stress.** For example, smart hydrogels may expand or contract depending on temperature, and drug-delivery systems may release medication in response to specific biological signals. **So, statement** **III is correct.** **Therefore**, **option (a) is the correct answer.**
**Relevance:** A news article advocates that India should become self-reliant in production of biomaterials, which may become the new frontier of materials engineering.

questions.subject: Polity
questions.questionText:
Which of the following statutory bodies, though
provided for under an Act of Parliament, has not
been formally constituted so far?

options.d: Insolvency and Bankruptcy Board of India

questions.explanationMarkdown:
● The **Arbitration Council of India** was envisaged under the **Arbitration and Conciliation (Amendment)** **Act, 2019** (amending the 1996 Act) to promote institutional arbitration in India. It was intended to grade
arbitral institutions, accredit arbitrators, maintain a repository of awards, and promote ADR mechanisms. However, despite being provided for in **2019**, it has **not been formally constituted**.
● The **Mediation Council of India** was established in **2023** under the **Mediation Act, 2023**, which provides a statutory framework for institutional, online, and community mediation. The Council regulates mediation service providers, registers mediators, lays down standards, and promotes mediation to reduce judicial pendency.
● The **National Financial Reporting Authority** was established in **2018** under **Section 132 of the** **Companies Act, 2013**. It functions as an independent regulator for accounting and auditing standards, monitors compliance, oversees auditors of large and listed companies, and has investigative and disciplinary powers.
● The Insolvency and Bankruptcy Board of India was established in **2016** under the **Insolvency and** **Bankruptcy Code, 2016**. It regulates insolvency professionals, agencies, and information utilities, frames regulations, and supervises time-bound insolvency resolution for individuals and corporate entities. **Therefore**, **option (a) is the correct answer.**
**Relevance:** The Supreme Court sought the Union government’s response on a plea seeking the constitution of the Arbitration Council.

questions.subject: Environment
questions.questionText:
With reference to the Wildlife (Protection) Act,
1972, consider the following statements:
I. 	 It gives the government legal authority and
procedures to manage and control situations
where wildlife and humans come into conflict.
II. 	 It contains specific provisions mandating
compensation to victims of wildlife attacks.
Which of the statements given above is/are
correct?
questions.explanationMarkdown:
● The **Wild Life (Protection) Act, 1972 (WLP Act)** is the primary legislation in India for the protection of wild animals, birds and plants. Over time, it has evolved to address emerging conservation challenges, **including human–wildlife conflict**, which has increased due to habitat fragmentation and developmental pressures.
● The Act provides legal authority and procedures to deal with situations involving conflict between humans and wildlife. **Under Section 11, the Chief Wildlife Warden may permit the hunting** (including capture or killing) **of a wild animal if it becomes dangerous to human life or property, or is diseased or** **disabled beyond recovery.** Additionally, the Act empowers authorities to declare protected areas and regulate activities therein, which indirectly helps in managing and mitigating human–wildlife conflict. **So,** **statement I is correct.**
● The Wildlife (Protection) Act, 1972 **does not contain specific statutory provisions mandating fixed** **compensation** for victims of wildlife attacks. Compensation for death, injury, crop damage, or livestock loss due to wildlife is generally provided through **state government ex-gratia schemes** and centrally sponsored programmes such as the Integrated Development of Wildlife Habitats. The parent Act itself does not prescribe fixed compensation amounts or establish a compensation tribunal. **So, statement II is** **not correct.** **Therefore**, **option (a) is the correct answer.**
**Relevance:** Prime Minister of India inaugurates flyover project in Kaziranga and one of the aims includes catering to man animal conflict.

questions.subject: Geography
questions.questionText:
With reference to ethanol production and blending
in India, consider the following statements:
I. 	 Maize has emerged as the largest feedstock
for ethanol production in recent years.
II. 	 Ethanol has a higher energy content than
petrol, and therefore increasing the proportion of ethanol in fuel improves a vehicle’s mileage.
Which of the statements given above is/are
correct?
questions.explanationMarkdown:
● **India’s Ethanol Blending Programme (EBP)** has undergone a structural shift in recent years, particularly after the advancement of the E20 target to 2025–26 and the expansion of permitted feedstocks.
● In recent years, **maize has emerged as a major** — and in some periods the largest — feedstock for ethanol production in India. Earlier, ethanol production was largely molasses-based (sugarcane by-product).
However, with policy incentives and expansion of grain-based distilleries, maize has significantly increased its share in ethanol production. **So, statement I is correct.**
● The **National Policy on Biofuels (2018)**, as amended in 2022, permits the use of **damaged foodgrains** **and surplus food stocks** for ethanol production, subject to approval by the National Biofuel Coordination Committee (NBCC). Therefore, there is no blanket prohibition on foodgrain use.
● **Ethanol has a lower calorific value (energy content per litre)** compared to petrol. As a result, increasing the proportion of ethanol in a fuel blend generally leads to a slight reduction in vehicle mileage (km per litre), not an increase. **So, statement II is not correct.** **Therefore**, **option (a) is the correct answer.**
**Relevance:** Ethanol blending creates ‘tension’ between food, energy security demands: Economics Survey 2025-

questions.subject: Economy
questions.questionText: The JALAJ Livelihood Centres recently inaugurated under the Namami Gange framework are:

options.a:
Decentralised centres established to integrate
river conservation with community-based sustainable livelihoods in the Ganga river basin
options.b:
Field-level units under the National Mission
for Clean Ganga for ecological restoration, biodiversity monitoring and community mobilisation along the Ganga
options.c:
Skill-development hubs set up exclusively
to train urban youth in water treatment and sewage management technologies
options.d:
Financial support platforms established to
compensate farmers affected by restrictions in Eco-Sensitive Zones along the Ganga river

questions.explanationMarkdown:
● The **JALAJ Livelihood Centres** have been launched under the **Namami Gange framework** as a joint initiative of the **National Mission for Clean Ganga (NMCG)** and the **Wildlife Institute of India (WII)**, aligned with the **Arth Ganga** concept. Arth Ganga seeks to create an economic linkage between river conservation and local livelihoods, recognising that sustainable rejuvenation of the Ganga requires active community participation.
● The centres aim to establish a **symbiotic relationship** between the river’s ecological health and the economic well-being of communities. Functioning on a **circular economy model**, they promote activities such as organic products, handicrafts, solar-based enterprises and eco-tourism, while encouraging the involvement of women and local groups like **Ganga Praharis**.
● Decentralised across several Ganga basin states, the initiative reinforces **Jan Bhagidari** in river conservation by combining ecological restoration with livelihood generation, thereby integrating environmental sustainability with grassroots development. **Therefore**, **option (a) is the correct answer.**
**Relevance:** Minister of Jal Shakti inaugurates 25 JALAJ Livelihood Centres

questions.subject: Polity
questions.sectionGroup: Part III, IV & IVA: Rights and Duties
questions.questionText:
The right to menstrual hygiene has been recognised
primarily as part of which of the following articles of the Constitution of India?

options.b:
Right to Life and Personal Liberty under
Article 21
options.c:
Right of special provisions for women under
Article 15
options.d:
Duty of the State to Improve Public Health
under Article 47

questions.explanationMarkdown:
● The Supreme Court, in **Dr. Jaya Thakur v. Government of India & Ors. (2026)**, held that the right to menstrual health and access to menstrual hygiene products and facilities is an integral component of the **Right to Life under Article 21 of the Constitution**. The Court observed that lack of access to safe and affordable menstrual hygiene directly undermines the **dignity, health and autonomy** of girls and women, and affects their ability to participate fully in education and society. This interpretation places menstrual hygiene within the constitutional guarantee of a life lived with dignity, rather than treating it merely as a policy or welfare concern.
● In its reasoning, the Court noted that inaccessibility of menstrual hygiene measures — such as sanitary products, functional gender-segregated toilets and safe disposal facilities — creates conditions of **humiliation, exclusion and avoidable suffering**, which are inconsistent with the guarantee of life and personal liberty under Article 21. By constitutionalising menstrual health, the Court emphasised the
State s **affirmative obligation** to ensure conditions that enable individuals, especially adolescent girls, to live without indignity and with equitable access to education and health. **Therefore**, **option (b) is the correct answer.**
**Relevance:** The Supreme Court of India, in the case of Dr. Jaya Thakur v. Government of India & Ors. (2026), officially recognized Menstrual Health and Hygiene as a fundamental right under Article 21**.**

questions.subject: Science & Technology
questions.microTopic: Information and Communication Technology, Robotics, AI
questions.questionText:
Consider the following statement:
**Statement I** In Quantum Key Distribution (QKD), any attempt at unauthorised interception of the key alters the quantum state and can be detected in real time. **Statement II** Quantum Communication allows faster-than-light transmission of information across long distances.
Which one of the following is correct in respect of the above statements?

options.a:
Both Statement I and Statement II are correct
and Statement II explains Statement I
options.b:
Both Statement I and Statement II are correct
but Statement II does not explain Statement I
options.c:
Statement I is correct but Statement II is
incorrect
options.d:
Statement I is incorrect but Statement II is
correct

questions.explanationMarkdown:
● Quantum Communication (QC) uses principles of quantum mechanics, such as superposition and entanglement, to ensure secure transmission of information. Its focus is on **security**, not faster-than-light communication.
● In Quantum Key Distribution (QKD), the secret key is sent using **quantum states** (tiny particles like photons that follow the rules of quantum mechanics). If someone tries to intercept or measure these quantum states, the act of measurement **changes their properties** because of the **Heisenberg Uncertainty** **Principle** (a rule that says certain properties of particles cannot be measured exactly at the same time) and the **no-cloning theorem** (which says quantum states cannot be copied exactly). This change can be noticed by the sender and receiver when they check parts of the key, letting them know that someone tried to eavesdrop. **So, statement I is correct.**
● However, while quantum communication uses phenomena like **quantum entanglement** (a connection between particles where changing one affects the other instantly), it **cannot be used to send actual** **information faster than light**. Even though the particles show instant correlations, we cannot control them to transmit a usable message. **So, statement II is not correct.** **Therefore**, **option (c) is the correct answer.**
**Relevance:** The rise of quantum computing introduces systemic risks to cybersecurity but also creates the opportunity to elevate cryptography as a strategic asset that enhances business resilience.

questions.subject: Geography
questions.questionText:
Which river in Northeast India, a major tributary
of the Brahmaputra River, is locally known as the “River of Blood” due to the reddish coloration of its waters caused by lateritic soil, supports the critically endangered White-bellied Heron, and is the site of the proposed 1,200-MW Kalai-II hydroelectric project?
questions.explanationMarkdown:
● The **Lohit River** is a **major eastern tributary of the Brahmaputra River**, originating in eastern **Tibet** and **flowing through Arunachal Pradesh**, particularly Anjaw district, before **joining the Brahmaputra** **in Assam**. It is locally referred to as the **“River of Blood”** because of the reddish hue of its waters, especially during the monsoon, **caused by the high content of lateritic soil carried downstream.**
● The river has recently been in focus due to the **proposed 1,200-MW Kalai-II hydroelectric project** **planned on its course.** The Lohit basin is also ecologically significant, as it **supports habitats of the** **critically endangered White-bellied Heron**, one of the rarest birds in the world. The species depends on relatively undisturbed, free-flowing riverine ecosystems, particularly stretches with rapids and adequate fish availability. Concerns have been raised regarding hydropower development in this basin because of its potential ecological implications. **Therefore**, **option (c) is the correct answer.**
**Relevance:** A Union Environment Ministry expert panel has recommended environmental clearance (EC) for the 1,200-MW Kalai-II hydroelectric project on Lohit River. **Subject**: Environment | Miscellaneous(env)

questions.subject: Science & Technology
questions.questionText:
With regards to India’s Gaganyaan programme,
consider the following pairs:
Mission Purpose
I. - Test Vehicle (TV-D2)
Testing parachute deployment and deceleration systems
II. - G2 Mission Testing in-orbit docking capability with a space station
III. - H1 Mission Flight carrying humanoid
robot Vyommitra to simulate human functions
How many of the pairs given above are correctly matched?
questions.explanationMarkdown:
● The objective of the **Gaganyaan programme** is to demonstrate **India’s capability to send astronauts** **to Low Earth Orbit (LEO) and safely return them to Earth.** The mission architecture includes a **human-rated launch vehicle (HLVM3)**, an Orbital Module comprising the Crew Module and Service Module, and a series of abort and recovery tests to ensure astronaut safety before the final crewed mission.
● **Test Vehicle (TV-D2)** is primarily meant **to validate the Crew Escape System and abort-related** **performance during ascent.** While parachute deployment and deceleration form part of recovery validation, the core objective of TV-D2 is testing abort capability rather than only parachute systems. **So,** **pair I is not correctly matched.**
● The **G2 mission** is an uncrewed orbital flight intended to validate spacecraft systems and **carry the** **humanoid robot Vyommitra to simulate human functions.** It is not designed to test in-orbit docking capability with a space station, as **docking is not part of the current Gaganyaan mission profile.** **So,** **pair II is not correctly matched.**
● The **H1 mission** is the final crewed flight that **will carry Indian astronaut-designates (Gaganyatris)** **into orbit.** It is not meant to carry Vyommitra, as the robot is part of uncrewed precursor missions. **So,** **pair III is not correctly matched.**
● Other important missions under the programme include the **Pad Abort Test (PAT)** to validate escape capability at the launch pad, the **Integrated Air Drop Test (IADT)** to test parachute and deceleration systems, the **G1 uncrewed orbital mission** to test the human-rated launch vehicle, and **Water Survival** **Test Facility (WSTF)** trials under the Gaganyaan which are being carried out by the **Indian Navy** in coordination with the Indian Space Research Organisation (ISRO). **Therefore**, **option (d) is the correct answer.**
**Relevance:** India's ambitious Gaganyaan programme is entering its final phase for the inaugural uncrewed mission (G1).

questions.subject: Science & Technology
questions.questionText:
The term “SWaDeS”, recently reported in the
context of defence preparedness, refers to which of the following?

options.a:
A satellite-based early warning system for
detecting underwater intrusions in coastal areas
options.b:
A portable sea water desalination system
developed for defence forces to generate potable water
options.c:
A strategic weapons deployment simulation
platform for tri-service commands
options.d:
A secure wireless data-sharing network for
naval and coastal surveillance units

questions.explanationMarkdown:
● The term **“SWaDeS” (Sea Water Desalination System)** refers to a **portable desalination technology** developed by the Defence Research and Development Organisation **(DRDO)** through its Defence Laboratory, Jodhpur. It has been designed **to meet the operational requirements of the armed forces** **deployed in coastal, island, and remote areas where access to potable water is limited.** The system converts seawater into drinking water, thereby enhancing field sustainability.
● The **engine-operated variant** can desalinate seawater with high salinity (around 35,000 mg/L TDS) to potable standards below 500 mg/L TDS and can cater to 20–25 soldiers. The **manually operated version** is lightweight, portable, and deployable by a single soldier, meeting emergency requirements of 10–12 personnel.
● By **enabling on-site water purification**, SWaDeS r**educes logistical dependence** and strengthens operational readiness of tri-service forces, particularly during naval operations, coastal deployments, and disaster response scenarios. **Therefore**, **option (b) is the correct answer.**
**Relevance:** DRDO Develops Portable SWaDeS System to Provide Drinking Water for Soldiers in Remote Areas.

questions.subject: History
questions.questionText:
Consider the following statements with regard to
records in Rajatarangini by Kalhana:
I. 	 Buddhism was prevalent in Kashmir much
before the reign of Emperor Ashoka.
II. 	 Zehanpora was a stupa containing relics of
Gautam Buddha.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
questions.explanationMarkdown:
● **Rajatarangini, the 12th-century chronicle by Kalhana**, indicates that **Buddhism was prevalent in** **Kashmir long before the reign of Emperor Ashoka.** This shows that the region had already developed Buddhist thought, monastic institutions, and places of worship, making it an early center of Buddhist learning in the subcontinent. Textual sources and historical references suggest that Kashmir played an important role in shaping Buddhist philosophy and supporting the growth of different sects, even prior to Ashoka’s patronage. **So, statement I is correct.**
● While Zehanpora in Kashmir is an important archaeological site with stupas dating to the Kushan era, there is no mention in **Rajatarangini that specifically identifies Zehanpora as a stupa containing relics** **of Gautam Buddha.** The identification of Zehanpora as a stupa comes from modern archaeological excavations, drone mapping, and historical comparisons with photographs preserved in foreign archives, not from Kalhana’s text. These excavations have, however, highlighted the site’s significance in understanding the spread of Mahayana Buddhism from Kashmir to Central Asia and China. **So, statement** **II is not correct.** **Therefore**, **option (a) is the correct answer.**
**Relevance:** 2000 year old stupa discovered by ASI at Zehanpora in Baramullah, Kashmir.

questions.subject: Geography
questions.questionText:
With reference to the phenomenon of ‘Bomb
Cyclone’, recently seen in the news, consider the following statements:
I. 	 It forms primarily due to the interaction
between contrasting air masses, often involving cold polar air and warm, moistureladen air.
II. 	 It is characterised by a rapid fall in central
air pressure of at least 24 millibars within 24 hours.
III. 	It is confined to tropical regions and derives
their energy mainly from warm ocean waters.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
questions.explanationMarkdown:
● A **Bomb Cyclone** is essentially **a rapidly intensifying mid-latitude (extratropical) cyclone** that undergoes a process known as **“explosive cyclogenesis.”** Such systems typically form **when cold, dense** **polar air collides with warm, moisture-laden air, usually over oceans where strong temperature** **gradients exist.** The sharp contrast between these air masses provides the necessary instability and latent heat release, leading to rapid storm development. **So, statement I is correct.**
● The **defining technical criterion** of a bomb cyclone is a fall in central atmospheric **pressure of at least** **24 millibars within a span of 24 hours.** This unusually rapid pressure drop strengthens the pressure gradient, generating intense winds, heavy snowfall, blizzards, or heavy rainfall depending on temperature conditions. **So, statement II is correct.**
● Bomb cyclones **are not confined to tropical regions; rather, they are characteristic of mid-latitude** **regions.** Unlike tropical cyclones, which derive their primary energy from warm ocean waters, bomb cyclones derive energy from strong horizontal temperature contrasts (baroclinic instability) between air masses. **So, statement III is not correct.** **Therefore**, **option (b) is the correct answer.**
**Relevance:** Winter Storm Ezra disrupted travel across the U.S. Northeast, while meteorologists warned that a rapidly intensifying bomb cyclone could further worsen conditions.
